import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title, Description } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import ColGroupMeta from '@/components/col-group/col-group.stories'
import { DocsLinkTo } from '@/_storybook/components';
import * as ColCheckboxButtonStories from './col-checkbox-button.stories';

<Meta of={ColCheckboxButtonStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Checkbox Button</Title>
    Checkboxes button are used for multiple choices, not for mutually exclusive choices. Each checkbox button works independently from other checkboxes button in the list, therefore checking an additional box does not affect any other selections.

    ## How to use it
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, ColCheckboxButton } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web component:
        ```typescript
        registerColibriComponents([ColCheckboxButton]);
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-checkbox-button>
          <col-icon name="emoji-circle" slot="icon"></col-icon>
          Checkbox button
        </col-checkbox-button>
        ```

    ## When to use it
    The "Checkbox Button" component is a common and versatile user interface element used to allow users to select one or more options from a list:

    - **Forms:** checkboxes are used to allow users to select multiple options from a list.
    - **Filtering and batch action:** Checkboxes are used to allow users to filter content by categories, attributes or tags.
    - **Settings:** Check boxes are used to allow users to enable or disable features, set language preferences, etc.
    - **Item selection and Lists with sub-selections:** In file management applications, checkboxes are used to allow users to select multiple items to perform actions such as delete, move or set the action according to the functionalities of their design.
    - Use the indeterminate state when the checkbox contains a sublist of selections, some of which are selected, and some unselected. In addition to default, selected, and indeterminate states, checkboxes also have states for hover, active, focus and disabled.


    ## Basic Usage
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={ColCheckboxButtonStories.Default} />

    ## States

    ### Checked State
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={ColCheckboxButtonStories.Checked} />

    ### Disabled States
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={ColCheckboxButtonStories.Disabled} />
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={ColCheckboxButtonStories.DisabledChecked} />

    ## Label Options

    The component offers multiple ways to add labels:

    ### With Default Slot
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={ColCheckboxButtonStories.WithDefaultSlot} />

    ### With Custom Label
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={ColCheckboxButtonStories.WithCustomLabel} />

    ## Icon Usage

    ### Icon Only
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={ColCheckboxButtonStories.OnlyIcon} />

    ### With Icon
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={ColCheckboxButtonStories.WithIcon} />

    <h3>Checkbox Button Group with ColGroup</h3>
    <p>See the <DocsLinkTo title={ColGroupMeta.title}>ColGroup</DocsLinkTo> documentation.</p>
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={ColCheckboxButtonStories.GroupCheckbox} />


    ### Interactive Example
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={ColCheckboxButtonStories.InteractiveExample} />

    ## Properties
    <ArgTypes of={ColCheckboxButtonStories} />

    ## Accessibility

    The checkbox button component follows accessibility best practices:

    - Uses proper ARIA attributes (`role="checkbox"`, `aria-checked`, `aria-disabled`)
    - Supports keyboard navigation and interaction (Space and Enter keys)
    - Maintains proper focus states
    - Provides appropriate visual states (checked, unchecked, disabled)

    ## Interact with the component

import { html, css } from 'lit';
import { ColibriStory, ColibriStoryMeta } from '@/types/storybook';
import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils/formatters';
import { fn } from '@storybook/test';
import { icons } from '@telesign/colibri-icons/icons-list';

type StoryArgs = {
  name: string;
  checked: boolean;
  disabled: boolean;
  label: string;
  customLabel: boolean;
  default: string;
  icon: string;
  change: () => void;
};

const meta = {
  title: 'Forms/Checkbox Button',
  component: 'col-checkbox-button',
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        excludeDecorators: true,
        transform: formatCodeString,
      },
    },
  },
  argTypes: {
    name: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'Name of the checkbox button',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: '""' },
        category: 'core',
      },
    },
    checked: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Controls the checked state of the checkbox button',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        category: 'core',
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'checked', neq: false },
    },
    disabled: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Disables the checkbox button',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        category: 'core',
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'disabled', neq: false },
    },
    customLabel: {
      name: 'customlabel',
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Enables custom label slot',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        category: 'core',
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'customlabel', neq: false },
    },
    default: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'The default content of the button (typically used for the label)',
      table: {
        category: 'slots',
        defaultValue: { summary: '""' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'default', neq: '' },
    },
    icon: {
      control: 'select',
      options: Object.values(icons),
      description: 'Icon content that appears before the label',
      table: {
        category: 'slots',
        defaultValue: { summary: '""' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'icon', neq: '' },
    },
    label: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'Custom label content (when customLabel is true)',
      table: {
        category: 'slots',
        defaultValue: { summary: '""' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'label', neq: '' },
    },
    change: {
      action: 'clicked',
      description: 'Fired when the checkbox state changes.',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
        type: { summary: '{ checked: boolean }' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'change', neq: fn() },
    },
  },
  args: {
    name: 'checkbox button',
    checked: false,
    disabled: false,
    label: '',
    customLabel: false,
    default: '',
    icon: '',
    change: fn(),
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<StoryArgs>;

export default meta;

type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

const styles = css`
  .checkbox-button-group {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 16px;
    font-family: var(--col-typography-font-family-primary);
  }

  .checkbox-button-group-header {
    margin-bottom: 12px;
  }

  .checkbox-button-group-content {
    display: flex;
    gap: 16px;
  }

    background-color: var(--col-colors-ui-primary-dark);
  }

  .toggle-button:active {
    background-color: var(--col-colors-ui-primary-dark);
  }
`;

const renderCheckboxButton: Story['render'] = args => html`
  <col-checkbox-button
    name=${args.name}
    ?checked=${args.checked}
    ?disabled=${args.disabled}
    ?customlabel=${args.customLabel}
  >
    <col-icon name=${args.icon} slot="icon"></col-icon>
    ${args.customLabel ? html`<span slot="label">${args.label}</span>` : args.default}
  </col-checkbox-button>
`;

export const Default: Story = {
  args: {
    checked: false,
    disabled: false,
    label: 'Checkbox button',
    icon: 'emoji-circle',
  },
  render: renderCheckboxButton,
};

  args: {
    checked: true,
    icon: 'check-circle',
    default: 'Checked state',
  },
  render: renderCheckboxButton,
};

  args: {
    disabled: true,
    default: 'Disabled state',
    icon: 'emoji-circle',
  },
  render: renderCheckboxButton,
};

  args: {
    disabled: true,
    checked: true,
    default: 'Disabled and checked',
    icon: 'check-circle',
  },
  render: renderCheckboxButton,
};

export const WithDefaultSlot: Story = {
  args: {
    default: 'Default slot label',
  },
  render: renderCheckboxButton,

  args: {
    customLabel: true,
    label: 'Custom formatted label',
  },
  render: renderCheckboxButton,
};

  args: {
    icon: 'emoji-circle',
  },

};

  args: {
    icon: 'emoji-circle',
    default: 'With icon',
  },
  render: renderCheckboxButton,
};

export const GroupCheckbox: Story = {
  args: {
    icon: 'emoji-circle',
    default: 'With icon',
  },
  render: args => html`
    <col-group label="Checkbox group button horizontal" role="group" withoutGap>
      <col-checkbox-button
        name="checkbox group button"
        ?checked=${args.checked}
        ?disabled=${args.disabled}
      >
        <col-icon name=${args.icon} slot="icon"></col-icon>
        ${args.customLabel ? html`<span slot="label">${args.label}</span>` : args.default}
      </col-checkbox-button>
      <col-checkbox-button>Week</col-checkbox-button>
      <col-checkbox-button>Month</col-checkbox-button>
      <col-checkbox-button>Year</col-checkbox-button>
    </col-group>
  `,
};

export const InteractiveExample: Story = {
  render: () => {
    let checked = false;

    const toggleState = () => {
      checked = !checked;
      const checkbox = document.querySelector('#controlled-checkbox-button') as HTMLInputElement;
      checkbox.checked = checked;
      const stateDisplay = document.querySelector('#state-display') as HTMLInputElement;
      stateDisplay.textContent = checked ? 'Checked' : 'Unchecked';
    };

    return html`
      <style>
        ${styles}
      </style>
      <div class="checkbox-button-group">
        <div class="checkbox-button-group-header">
          <strong>Current state: </strong><span id="state-display">Unchecked</span>
        </div>
        <div class="checkbox-button-group-content">
          <col-checkbox-button id="controlled-checkbox-button" @change=${toggleState}>
            <col-icon name="check-circle" slot="icon"></col-icon>
            Click to toggle state
          </col-checkbox-button>
        </div>
        <div>
          <button class="toggle-button" @click=${toggleState}>Toggle state externally</button>
        </div>
      </div>
    `;
  },
};

FILE: src/components/col-checkbox-card/col-checkbox-card.mdx

import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import * as ColCheckboxCardStories from './col-checkbox-card.stories';

<Meta of={ColCheckboxCardStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Checkbox Card</Title>
    Checkboxes card are used for multiple choices, not for mutually exclusive choices. Each checkbox card works independently from other checkboxes card in the list, therefore checking an additional box does not affect any other selections.

    ## How to use it
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, ColCheckboxCard } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web component:
        ```typescript
        registerColibriComponents([ColCheckboxCard]);
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-checkbox-card title="Card Title" description="Card description text">
        ...
        </col-checkbox-card>
        ```

    ## When to use it
    The "Checkbox Button" component is a common and versatile user interface element used to allow users to select one or more options from a list:

    - **Forms:** checkboxes are used to allow users to select multiple options from a list.
    - **Filtering and batch action:** Checkboxes are used to allow users to filter content by categories, attributes or tags.
    - **Settings:** Check boxes are used to allow users to enable or disable features, set language preferences, etc.
    - **Item selection and Lists with sub-selections:** In file management applications, checkboxes are used to allow users to select multiple items to perform actions such as delete, move or set the action according to the functionalities of their design.
    - Use the indeterminate state when the checkbox contains a sublist of selections, some of which are selected, and some unselected. In addition to default, selected, and indeterminate states, checkboxes also have states for hover, active, focus and disabled.


    <h2>Usage</h2>
    <p>
      The Checkbox Card component enables users to select content presented in a card format.
      It combines the functionality of a checkbox with a visual card container, making
      selections more engaging and providing more space for content.
    </p>

    <h2>Examples</h2>

    <h3>Default</h3>
    <p>Standard checkbox card with title and description.</p>
    <Canvas of={ColCheckboxCardStories.Default} />

    <h3>Checked State</h3>
    <p>Checkbox card in checked state.</p>
    <Canvas of={ColCheckboxCardStories.Checked} />

    <h3>Disabled State</h3>
    <p>Checkbox card in disabled state.</p>
    <Canvas of={ColCheckboxCardStories.Disabled} />

    <h3>Custom Content</h3>
    <p>Checkbox card with custom content instead of the default title/description structure.</p>
    <Canvas of={ColCheckboxCardStories.CustomContent} />

    <h3>Disabled Checked State</h3>
    <p>Checkbox card in disabled and checked state.</p>
    <Canvas of={ColCheckboxCardStories.DisabledChecked} />

    <h3>Indeterminate State</h3>
    <p>Checkbox card in indeterminate state, useful for parent-child selection scenarios.</p>
    <Canvas of={ColCheckboxCardStories.Indeterminate} />

    <h3>With Custom Icon</h3>
    <p>Checkbox card with a custom icon instead of the default checkmark.</p>
    <Canvas of={ColCheckboxCardStories.WithCustomIcon} />

    <h3>Card Group</h3>
    <p>Multiple checkbox cards displayed in a grid layout for feature selection.</p>
    <Canvas of={ColCheckboxCardStories.CardGroup} />

    <h3>Custom Content Group</h3>

    <Canvas of={ColCheckboxCardStories.CustomContentGroup} />

    <h2>Properties</h2>

  </div>
</div>

import { html, css, nothing } from 'lit';
import { ColibriStory, ColibriStoryMeta } from '@/types/storybook';
import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils/formatters';
import { icons } from '@telesign/colibri-icons/icons-list';

type StoryArgs = {
  name: string;
  checked: boolean;
  disabled: boolean;
  title: string;
  description: string;
  customContent: boolean;
  indeterminate: boolean;
  withIcon: boolean;
  iconName: string;
};

const meta = {
  title: 'Forms/Checkbox Card',
  component: 'col-checkbox-card',
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        excludeDecorators: true,
        transform: formatCodeString,
      },
    },
  },
  argTypes: {
    name: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'Name of the checkbox card',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: '""' },
        category: 'core',
      },
    },
    checked: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Controls the checked state of the checkbox card',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
        category: 'core',
      },
      if: { arg: 'checked', neq: false },
    },
    disabled: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Disables the checkbox card',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        category: 'core',
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'disabled', neq: false },
    },
    title: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'Title text for the checkbox card',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        category: 'core',
        defaultValue: { summary: '""' },
      },
    },
    description: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'Description text for the checkbox card',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        category: 'core',
        defaultValue: { summary: '""' },
      },
    },
    customContent: {
      name: 'customcontent',
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Enables custom content slot',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
        category: 'core',
      },
      if: { arg: 'customContent', neq: false },
    },
    indeterminate: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Sets the checkbox card to an indeterminate state',
      table: {
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
        category: 'core',
      },
      if: { arg: 'indeterminate', neq: false },
    },
    withIcon: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description:
        'Whether to show an icon when the checkbox card is checked. **Storybook control only, not a component prop.**',
      table: {
        category: 'Storybook',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'withIcon', neq: false },
    },
    iconName: {
      control: 'select',
      options: icons,
      description:
        'Name of the icon to show when the checkbox card is checked. **Storybook control only, not a component prop.**',
      table: {
        category: 'Storybook',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: '""' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'withIcon', neq: false },
    },

  args: {
    name: 'Checkbox Card',
    checked: false,
    disabled: false,
    title: 'Card Title',
    description: 'Card description text',
    customContent: false,
    indeterminate: false,
    withIcon: false,
    iconName: '',
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<StoryArgs>;

export default meta;

type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

const styles = css`
  .checkbox-card-group {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 16px;
    font-family: var(--col-typography-font-family-primary);
  }

  .checkbox-card-group-vertical {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;

    font-family: var(--col-typography-font-family-primary);
  }

  .checkbox-card-group-header {
    margin-bottom: 12px;
  }

  .toggle-button {
    padding: 8px 16px;
    background-color: var(--col-colors-ui-primary);

    font-family: var(--col-typography-font-family-primary);
    font-size: 14px;
  }

  .toggle-button:hover {
    background-color: var(--col-colors-ui-primary-dark);
  }

  .toggle-button:active {
    background-color: var(--col-colors-ui-primary-dark);
  }
`;

const RenderCheckboxCard: Story['render'] = ({
  checked,
  disabled,
  title,
  description,
  name,
  indeterminate,
  withIcon,
  iconName,
}) => html`
  <col-checkbox-card
    name=${name}
    title=${title}
    description=${description}
    ?checked=${checked}
    ?disabled=${disabled}
    ?indeterminate=${indeterminate}
  >
    ${withIcon
      ? html`<col-icon name=${iconName} slot="checked-icon" size="12" color="white"></col-icon>`
      : nothing}
  </col-checkbox-card>
`;

export const Default: Story = {
  args: {
    title: 'Card Title',
    description: 'Card description text',
  },
  render: RenderCheckboxCard,
};

export const Checked: Story = {
  args: {
    checked: true,
  },
  render: RenderCheckboxCard,
};

export const Disabled: Story = {
  args: {
    disabled: true,
  },
  render: RenderCheckboxCard,
};

export const DisabledChecked: Story = {
  args: {
    disabled: true,
    checked: true,
  },
  render: RenderCheckboxCard,
};

export const Indeterminate: Story = {
  args: {
    indeterminate: true,
  },
  render: RenderCheckboxCard,
};

export const WithCustomIcon: Story = {
  args: {
    withIcon: true,
    iconName: 'emoji-circle',
  },
  render: RenderCheckboxCard,
};

export const CustomContent: Story = {
  args: {
    customContent: true,
  },
  render: ({ checked, disabled, customContent }) => html`
    <col-checkbox-card ?checked=${checked} ?disabled=${disabled} ?customcontent=${customContent}>
      <div style="padding: 8px 0;">
        <h3 style="margin: 0 0 8px 0;">Custom Content</h3>
        <p style="margin: 0;">You can add any custom content here</p>
      </div>
    </col-checkbox-card>
  `,
};

export const CardGroup: Story = {
  parameters: {
    __sb: {
      display: 'grid',
      gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(3, 1fr)',
      gap: '16px',
    },
  },
  render: () => html`
    <col-checkbox-card
      name="feature1"
      title="Feature 1"
      description="Essential feature for basic users"
    ></col-checkbox-card>
    <col-checkbox-card
      name="feature2"
      title="Feature 2"
      description="Advanced feature for power users"
    ></col-checkbox-card>
    <col-checkbox-card
      name="feature3"
      title="Feature 3"
      description="Premium feature for enterprise users"
    ></col-checkbox-card>
  `,
};

export const CustomContentGroup: Story = {
  parameters: {
    __sb: {
      display: 'grid',
      gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(2, 1fr)',
    },
  },
  render: () => html`
    <col-checkbox-card name="theme1" customcontent>
      <div style="padding: 16px 0; text-align: center;">
        <div style="font-size: 24px; margin-bottom: 8px;">☀️</div>
        <h3 style="margin: 0 0 8px 0;">Light Theme</h3>
        <p style="margin: 0;">Bright and clean interface</p>
      </div>
    </col-checkbox-card>
    <col-checkbox-card name="theme2" customcontent>
      <div style="padding: 16px 0; text-align: center;">
        <div style="font-size: 24px; margin-bottom: 8px;">🌙</div>
        <h3 style="margin: 0 0 8px 0;">Dark Theme</h3>
        <p style="margin: 0;">Easy on the eyes at night</p>
      </div>
    </col-checkbox-card>
  `,
};

export const InteractiveExample: Story = {
  render: () => {
    let checked = false;

    const toggleState = () => {
      checked = !checked;
      const checkbox = document.querySelector('#controlled-checkbox-card') as HTMLInputElement;
      checkbox.checked = checked;
      const stateDisplay = document.querySelector('#state-display') as HTMLInputElement;
      stateDisplay.textContent = checked ? 'Checked' : 'Unchecked';
    };

    return html`
      <style>
        ${styles}
      </style>
      <div class="checkbox-card-group">
        <div class="checkbox-card-group-header">
          <strong>Current state: </strong><span id="state-display">Unchecked</span>
        </div>
        <div class="checkbox-card-group-content">
          <col-checkbox-card
            title="Checkbox Card"
            description="Checkbox Card description"
            id="controlled-checkbox-card"
            @change=${toggleState}
          >
          </col-checkbox-card>
        </div>
        <div>
          <button class="toggle-button" @click=${toggleState}>Toggle state externally</button>
        </div>
      </div>
    `;
  },
};

import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import { Callout } from '@/_storybook/components';
import * as ColDialogStories from './col-dialog.stories';

<Meta of={ColDialogStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Dialog</Title>
    The `col-dialog` component is a pop-up window that requests the user's atention to perform a specific action or provide important information.

    <Subtitle>Table of contents</Subtitle>
    - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
      - [Installation](#installation)
    - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
      - [Best Practices](#best-practices)
    - [Component preview](#component-preview)
      - [Basic Dialog](#basic-dialog)
      - [Custom Width and Height Dialog](#custom-width-and-height-dialog)
      - [Small Dialog with No Footer](#small-dialog-with-no-footer)
      - [Medium Dialog with Prevent Close](#medium-dialog-with-prevent-close)
      - [Multiple Dialogs](#multiple-dialogs)
      - [With Header and Footer](#with-header-and-footer)
      - [Full Integration Example](#full-integration-example)
    - [Properties](#properties)
    - [Accessibility](#accessibility)
    - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

    ## How to use it

    ### Installation
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, ColDialog, ColModalHeader, ColModalFooter } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColDialog, ColModalHeader, ColModalFooter]);

        // Or register all components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-dialog>
          <col-modal-header slot="header" title="Dialog Title"></col-modal-header>

          <p>Basic paragraph to see the dialog content</p>

          <col-modal-footer slot="footer">
            <col-button slot="actions" data-action="cancel" color="secondary">Cancel</col-button>
            <col-button slot="actions" data-action="confirm" color="primary">Save</col-button>
          </col-modal-footer>
        </col-dialog>
        ```

    ## When to use it

    The dialog component is a pop-up window that requests the user's attention to perform a specific action or provide important information. The following are some common use cases for the Dialog component:

    * **Confirmation of Actions**: When a user attempts to delete an item, the dialog may request a confirmation to ensure that the action was not performed in error.
    * **Warnings and Alerts**: Inform the user about possible risks or consequences of an action, such as downloading files from untrusted sources.
    * **Forms and Data Entry**: Provide a pop-up form for registration or login without redirecting the user to a new page.
    * **Additional Information**: Display additional information about a product without redirecting the user away from the main page.
    * **Choice of Options**: Allow the user to select specific preferences or settings in a controlled environment.
    * **Terms and Conditions**: Display the terms and conditions that the user must accept before proceeding with a specific action.
    * **Feedback and Surveys**: Ask users to provide comments or ratings about their experience.
    * **Authentication and Authorization**: Ask the user to confirm their identity using a verification code or two-factor authentication.
    * **Processes and Workflows**: Show the progress of an ongoing task and provide step-by-step instructions for completing a task or configuration.

    ### Best Practices

    * **Use dialogs sparingly**: Don’t overuse dialogs. They are disruptive and can easily annoy the user if used incorrectly or too frequently.
    * **Dialogs should be user initiated**: A user action, such as clicking a button, should trigger the dialog to open. Don’t interrupt the user by opening a dialog when they aren’t expecting it.
    * **Keep dialog tasks simple and focused**: Use dialog for secondary actions or content that doesn't require a full page navigation.
    * **Appropriate sizing**: Choose the dialog size and layout based on content needs (passive without footer for notifications or transactional for form handling with or without header).
    * **Focus management**: The dialog automatically traps focus when open, ensuring keyboard navigation stays within the Dialog.
    * **Escape hatch**: Always provide a clear way to close the dialog (close button, backdrop click, or Escape key).
    * **Responsive design**: Despite the device you are using, the dialog will fit according to the screen no matter the size, width or height values.
    * **Animation**: The Dialog includes smooth slide-in/out animations for better user experience.

    ## Component preview
    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    ### Basic Dialog
    A simple dialog with default layout (with header and footer) and default small size. For example purposes, the default banner is the one shown in the [Installation](#how-to-use-it) example.

    <Canvas of={ColDialogStories.Default} />

    - It's expected for the header and footer slots to use `col-modal-header` and `col-modal-footer` but you can use your own implementation with other HTML tags.
    - It's not mandatory to have header, content or footer. In the further examples you will see how the Dialog looks without one or more of these slots.

    #### Considerations
    In order to dispatch close and action events correctly you must add the `data-action` attribute to the buttons you use (whether you use `col-button`, `button` or other interactive HTML tag).
    - **dialog-close**: Only dispatched by the header. Must have `data-action=close` value. For `col-modal-header` it already has a close button so there is no need of the `data-action` attribute.
    ```html
    <!-- Modal Header example implementation -->
    <col-modal-header slot="header" title="Dialog Title"></col-modal-header>

    <!-- Custom example implementation -->
    <div slot="header" style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between">
      <h4>Dialog Title</h4>
      <button data-action="close" type="button"> Close </button>
    </div>
    ```
    - **dialog-action**: Only dispatched by the footer. Must have `data-action=cancel` and/or `data-action=confirm` value.
    ```html
    <!-- Modal Footer example implementation -->
    <col-modal-footer slot="footer">
      <col-button slot="actions" data-action="cancel" color="secondary">Cancel</col-button>
      <col-button slot="actions" data-action="confirm" color="primary">Confirm</col-button>
    </col-modal-footer>

    <!-- Custom example implementation -->
    <div slot="footer" style="display: flex; justify-content: end">
      <button data-action="cancel">Cancel</button>
      <button data-action="confirm">Confirm</button>
    </div>
    ```

    ### Custom Width and Height Dialog
    A dialog with no footer is considered a Passive Dialog which only presents information to the user with further action besides the header close button
    <Canvas of={ColDialogStories.CustomWidthHeight} />

    ### Small Dialog with No Footer
    A dialog with no footer is considered a Passive Dialog which only presents information to the user with further action besides the header close button
    <Canvas of={ColDialogStories.SmallNoFooter} />
    - Despite having a footer slot it won't show because of the `layout="nofooter"` attribute.

    ### Medium Dialog with Prevent Close
    A dialog with the `preventclose` property won't allow the user to close it by clicking outside of the dialog or pressing the `Escape` key.
    <Canvas of={ColDialogStories.MediumPreventClose} />

    ### Large Dialog with No Header
    A dialog with no header is considered a Transactional Dialog which requests the user to complete and action.
    <Canvas of={ColDialogStories.LargeNoHeader} />
    - Despite having a header slot it won't show because of the `layout="noheader"` attribute.

    ### Multiple Dialogs
    It's posible to have two dialogs open at the same time. With the property `keepopen` you define the dialog you want to keep open until an action is completed and changes the value of the property.

    In this example we can see nested Dialogs, meaning a Dialog that can open another Dialog. To see it, click the 'Save' button on the first Dialog (seen when you click on 'Open Dialog').
    <Canvas of={ColDialogStories.Multiple} />

    ### Full Integration Example
    The purpose of this example is to show real life scenarios to use and test the `col-dialog` component.
    - Confirmation of deletion for elements
    - Submit data from forms within the components
    <Canvas of={ColDialogStories.FullIntegration} />

    ## Properties
    <ArgTypes of={ColDialogStories} />

    ## Accessibility

    The Dialog component is built with accessibility in mind:

    * **Focus trap**: When open, focus is trapped within the Dialog to prevent keyboard navigation outside.
    * **ARIA attributes**: Proper ARIA labels and roles for screen readers.
    * **Keyboard navigation**:
      - `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` to navigate through focusable elements.
      - `Escape` to close the Dialog (when not persistent).
    * **Backdrop click**: Clicking outside the Dialog closes it (depending on preventClose property).
    * **Scroll lock**: Body scroll is disabled when Dialog is open to prevent background scrolling.
    * **Hide on action**: Despite the action executed (close, cancel, confirm) the dialog will automatically be inactive.

    ## CSS Custom Properties

    You can customize the dialog width and height using CSS custom properties:

    ```css
    col-dialog {
      --dialog-width: 500px; /* Custom width */
      --dialog-height: 400px; /* Custom height */
    }
    ```

    ## JavaScript API

    ### Opening and Closing Programmatically

    ```javascript
    // Get dialog element
    const dialog = document.querySelector('col-dialog');

    // Open dialog
    dialog.active = true;
    // or
    dialog.setAttribute('active', 'true');

    // Close dialog
    dialog.active = false;
    // or
    dialog.removeAttribute('active');

    // Listen for close events
    dialog.addEventListener('dialog-close', (e) => {
      console.log('dialog closed by clicking on the header close button');
    });

    dialog.addEventListener('dialog-action', (e) => {
      console.log('dialog closed by clicking one of the footer buttons');
    });

    dialog.addEventListener('dialog-prevented', (e) => {
      console.log('dialog prevented from closing by clicking or pressing Esc key on the overlay');
    });
    ```

    <Subtitle>Interact with the component</Subtitle>
    To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={ColDialogStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.

  </div>
</div>

import { html } from 'lit';
import type { ColibriStoryMeta, ColibriStory } from '@/types/storybook';
import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils/formatters';
import { fn } from '@storybook/test';

type StoryArgs = {
  active: boolean;
  layout: 'default' | 'noheader' | 'nofooter';
  size: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large' | 'full';
  height?: number;
  width?: number;
  preventClose: boolean;
  keepOpen: boolean;
  onDialogClose: () => void;
  onDialogPrevented: () => void;
  onDialogAction: () => void;
};

const meta = {
  title: 'Overlays/Dialog',
  component: 'col-dialog',
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        excludeDecorators: true,
        transform: formatCodeString,
      },
    },
    __sb: {
      minHeight: '400px',
    },
  },
  argTypes: {
    active: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Controls whether the drawer is visible',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
    },
    layout: {
      control: 'select',
      options: ['default', 'noheader', 'nofooter'],
      description: 'Defines whether the dialog has no header, no footer or contains both',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: "'default' | 'noheader' | 'nofooter'" },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'default' },
      },
    },
    size: {
      control: 'select',
      options: ['small', 'medium', 'large', 'full'],
      description:
        'Preset dialog width (small: 448px, medium: 640px, large: 960px, full: 1280px) and height (small, medium, large: 311px, full: 720px)',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: "'small' | 'medium' | 'large' | 'full'" },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'small' },
      },
    },
    width: {
      control: 'number',
      description: 'Custom width in pixels (overrides size preset)',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'number' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'undefined' },
      },
    },
    height: {
      control: 'number',
      description: 'Custom height in pixels (overrides size preset)',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'number' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'undefined' },
      },
    },
    preventClose: {
      name: 'preventclose',
      control: 'boolean',
      description:
        'Defines whether the dialog is allow to close by clicking or pressing Escape key on the overlay ',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
    },
    keepOpen: {
      name: 'keepopen',
      control: 'boolean',
      description:
        'Defines whether the dialog closes at submit or waits for another action to be completed',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
    },
    onDialogAction: {
      action: 'dialog-action',
      description: 'Fired for cancel and confirm actions within the dialog',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
      },
    },
    onDialogClose: {
      action: 'dialog-close',
      description: 'Fired when dialog closes',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
      },
    },
    onDialogPrevented: {
      action: 'dialog-prevented',
      description: 'Fired when a close action is prevented',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
      },
    },
  },
  args: {
    active: false,
    layout: 'default',
    size: 'small',
    keepOpen: false,
    onDialogPrevented: fn(),
    onDialogClose: fn(),
    onDialogAction: fn(),
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<StoryArgs>;

export default meta;

type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

/**
 * Default story showing a basic dialog
 */
export const Default: Story = {
  render: ({
    active,
    layout,
    size,
    width,
    height,
    preventClose,
    keepOpen,
    onDialogAction,
    onDialogClose,
    onDialogPrevented,
  }) => {
    const ID = `basic-dialog-${new Date().getTime()}`;
    const handleButtonClick = () => {
      const dialog = document.getElementById(ID);
      if (dialog) {
        dialog.setAttribute('active', 'true');
      }
    };

    return html`
      <div>
        <col-button color="primary" @click=${handleButtonClick}>
          Click me to open a Dialog
        </col-button>

        <col-dialog
          id=${ID}
          ?active=${active}
          layout=${layout}
          size=${size}
          ?preventclose=${preventClose}
          width=${width || null}
          height=${height || null}
          ?keepopen=${keepOpen}
          @dialog-action=${onDialogAction}
          @dialog-close=${onDialogClose}
          @dialog-prevented=${onDialogPrevented}
        >
          <col-modal-header slot="header" title="Dialog"></col-modal-header>

          <p>Basic paragraph to see the dialog content</p>

          <col-modal-footer slot="footer">
            <col-button slot="actions" data-action="cancel" color="secondary">Cancel</col-button>
            <col-button slot="actions" data-action="confirm" color="primary">Save</col-button>
          </col-modal-footer>
        </col-dialog>
      </div>
    `;
  },
};

export const Multiple: Story = {
  args: {
    size: 'medium',
    keepOpen: true,
  },
  render: args => {
    const ID1 = `primary-multiple-dialog-${new Date().getTime()}`;
    const ID2 = `secondary-multiple-dialog-${new Date().getTime()}`;
    const handleButtonClick = () => {
      const primaryDialog = document.getElementById(ID1);
      if (primaryDialog) {
        primaryDialog.setAttribute('active', 'true');
      }
    };

    const handleSaveButtonClick = () => {
      const secondaryDialog = document.getElementById(ID2);
      if (secondaryDialog) {
        secondaryDialog.setAttribute('active', 'true');
      }
    };

    const handleConfirmButtonClick = () => {
      const primaryDialog = document.getElementById(ID1) as HTMLElement;
      if (primaryDialog) {
        primaryDialog.removeAttribute('active');
      }
    };

    return html`
      <div>
        <col-button color="primary" @click=${handleButtonClick}>
          Click me to open a Dialog
        </col-button>

        <col-dialog
          id=${ID1}
          ?active=${args.active}
          layout=${args.layout}
          size=${args.size}
          width=${args.width || null}
          height=${args.width || null}
          ?keepopen=${args.keepOpen}
          @dialog-action=${args.onDialogAction}
          @dialog-close=${args.onDialogClose}
          @dialog-prevented=${args.onDialogPrevented}
        >
          <col-modal-header slot="header" title="Transactional Dialog"></col-modal-header>

          <p>
            Requires an action to be taken in order for the modal to be completed and closed.
            Contains a cancel and primary action buttons.
          </p>
          <form>
            <col-text-field id="email" name="email" label="Email" required></col-text-field>
          </form>

          <col-modal-footer slot="footer">
            <col-button slot="actions" data-action="cancel">Cancel</col-button>
            <col-button
              slot="actions"
              data-action="confirm"
              color="primary"
              @click=${handleSaveButtonClick}
            >
              Save
            </col-button>
          </col-modal-footer>
        </col-dialog>

        <col-dialog id=${ID2} width="300" height="180">
          <col-modal-header slot="header" title="Dialog Title"></col-modal-header>
          <p>Click Confirm button to submit your data</p>
          <col-modal-footer slot="footer">
            <col-button
              slot="actions"
              data-action="confirm"
              color="primary"
              @click=${handleConfirmButtonClick}
            >
              Confirm
            </col-button>
          </col-modal-footer>
        </col-dialog>
      </div>
    `;
  },
};

export const FullIntegration: Story = {
  render: args => {
    const ID1 = `primary-full-dialog-${new Date().getTime()}`;
    const ID2 = `secondary-full-dialog-${new Date().getTime()}`;

    const handleButtonClick = () => {
      const primaryDialog = document.getElementById(ID1);
      if (primaryDialog) {
        primaryDialog.setAttribute('active', 'true');
      }
    };

    const handleSaveButtonClick = () => {
      const form = document.getElementById('dialog-form') as HTMLFormElement;
      const avatar = document.getElementById('user-avatar');
      const formData = new FormData(form);
      const data = Object.fromEntries(formData.entries());
      avatar?.setAttribute('name', data.username);
    };

    const handleNotifications = () => {
      const toggle = document.getElementById('enable-notifications-toggle');
      if (toggle?.hasAttribute('checked')) {
        const secondaryDialog = document.getElementById(ID2);
        if (secondaryDialog) {
          secondaryDialog.setAttribute('active', 'true');
        }
      }
    };

    const handleConfirmDialog = e => {
      const action = e.detail.action;
      const toggle = document.getElementById('enable-notifications-toggle');
      if (action === 'footer-confirm') {
        toggle?.removeAttribute('checked');

        const secondaryDialog = document.getElementById(ID2);
        if (secondaryDialog) {
          secondaryDialog.removeAttribute('active');
        }
      }
      if (action === 'footer-cancel' || e.type === 'dialog-close') {
        toggle?.setAttribute('checked', 'true');
      }
    };

    return html`
      <div>
        <h3>Account Settings</h3>
        <div style="width: 400px;">
          <col-typography variant="subheading">Personal Data</col-typography>
          <div style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between;">
            <col-avatar id="user-avatar" name="John Doe" variant="full"></col-avatar>
            <col-button color="primary" @click=${handleButtonClick} color="primary"
              >Change Username</col-button
            >
          </div>
          <col-dialog
            id=${ID1}
            ?active=${args.active}
            layout=${args.layout}
            size=${args.size}
            width=${args.width || null}
            height=${args.width || null}
            ?keepopen=${args.keepOpen}
            @dialog-action=${args.onDialogAction}
            @dialog-close=${args.onDialogClose}
            @dialog-prevented=${args.onDialogPrevented}
          >
            <col-modal-header slot="header" title="Change Personal Data"></col-modal-header>

            <p>
              Please fill the text input in order to change your username. Then click the Save
              button in order to continue.
            </p>
            <form id="dialog-form">
              <col-text-field
                id="username"
                name="username"
                label="Username"
                required
              ></col-text-field>
            </form>

            <col-modal-footer slot="footer">
              <col-button slot="actions" data-action="cancel"> Cancel </col-button>
              <col-button
                slot="actions"
                data-action="confirm"
                color="primary"
                @click=${handleSaveButtonClick}
              >
                Save
              </col-button>
            </col-modal-footer>
          </col-dialog>
        </div>
        <div style="margin-top: 10px;">
          <col-typography variant="subheading">Account Preferences</col-typography>
          <col-group orientation="vertical">
            <col-toggle
              checked
              name="enable-notifications-toggle"
              id="enable-notifications-toggle"
              @change=${handleNotifications}
            >
              Enable Notifications
            </col-toggle>
          </col-group>
          <col-dialog
            id=${ID2}
            width="300"
            height="180"
            @dialog-close=${handleConfirmDialog}
            @dialog-action=${handleConfirmDialog}
          >
            <col-modal-header slot="header" title="Dialog Title"></col-modal-header>
            <p>Are you sure you want to disable notifications for your account?</p>
            <col-modal-footer slot="footer">
              <col-button slot="actions" data-action="cancel"> Cancel </col-button>
              <col-button slot="actions" data-action="confirm" color="primary">
                Confirm
              </col-button>
            </col-modal-footer>
          </col-dialog>
        </div>
      </div>
    `;
  },
};

import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import * as ColDividerStories from './col-divider.stories';

<Meta of={ColDividerStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Divider</Title>
    The Colibri Divider component is used to group content into lists or other containers.

    <Subtitle>How to use it</Subtitle>
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, registerAllComponents, ColDivider } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Option 1: Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColDivider]);

        // Option 2: Register all Colibri components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        // Horizontal divider by default
        <col-divider></col-divider>

        // Vertical divider
        <col-divider orientation="vertical"></col-divider>
        ```

    <Subtitle>When to use it</Subtitle>
    Dividers are thin lines that group content into lists or other containers.

    - Make dividers visible but not bold
    - Only use dividers if items can't be grouped with open space
    - Use dividers to group things, not separate individual items

    <Subtitle>Component preview</Subtitle>
    You can click on the **Show code** button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component:

    <h4>Orientation: Horizontal (default)</h4>
    <Canvas of={ColDividerStories.Default}/>

    <h4>Orientation: Vertical</h4>
    <Canvas of={ColDividerStories.Vertical} />

    <Subtitle>Properties</Subtitle>

    <ArgTypes of={ColDividerStories} />

    <Subtitle>Interact with the component</Subtitle>
    To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={ColDividerStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.

  </div>
</div>

FILE: src/components/col-divider/col-divider.stories.tsx

import { html } from 'lit';
import type { ColibriStoryMeta, ColibriStory } from '@/types/storybook';
import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils/formatters';
import { DIVIDER_ORIENTATIONS } from '@telesign/colibri';

type StoryArgs = {
  orientation: string;
};

const meta = {
  title: 'Helpers/Divider',
  component: 'col-divider',
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        excludeDecorators: true,
        transform: formatCodeString,
      },
    },
  },
  argTypes: {
    orientation: {
      control: 'select',
      options: Object.values(DIVIDER_ORIENTATIONS),
      description: 'The orientation of the divider (horizontal or vertical)',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'horizontal' },
      },
    },
  },
  args: {
    orientation: DIVIDER_ORIENTATIONS.HORIZONTAL,
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<StoryArgs>;

export default meta;

type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

/**
 * Default story showing a divider with horizontal orientation
 * Use the controls panel to see a divider with vertical orientation
 */
export const Default: Story = {
  render: args => html` <col-divider orientation=${args.orientation}></col-divider> `,
};

/**
 * Story showing a divider with a vertical orientation
 */
export const Vertical: Story = {
  args: {
    orientation: DIVIDER_ORIENTATIONS.VERTICAL,
  },
  parameters: {
    __sb: {
      display: 'flex',
      justifyContent: 'center',
      height: '400px',
      width: '100%',
    },
  },
  render: args => html` <col-divider orientation=${args.orientation}></col-divider> `,
};

FILE: src/components/col-drawer/col-drawer.mdx
================================================
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import { Callout } from '@/_storybook/components';
import * as ColDrawerStories from './col-drawer.stories';

<Meta of={ColDrawerStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Drawer</Title>
    The Drawer component is a sliding panel that overlays the main content from the edge of the viewport. It's commonly used for navigation menus, forms, filters, or displaying supplementary content without navigating away from the current page.

    <Subtitle>Table of contents</Subtitle>
    - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
      - [Installation](#installation)
      - [Best Practices](#best-practices)
    - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
    - [Component preview](#component-preview)
      - [Basic Drawer](#basic-drawer)
      - [Left Position](#left-position)
      - [Different Sizes](#different-sizes)
      - [With Header and Footer](#with-header-and-footer)
      - [Navigation Drawer](#navigation-drawer)
      - [Form Drawer](#form-drawer)
    - [Properties](#properties)
    - [Events](#events)
    - [Accessibility](#accessibility)
    - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

    ## How to use it

    ### Installation
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, ColDrawer, ColOverlay, ColModalHeader, ColModalFooter } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColDrawer, ColOverlay, ColModalHeader, ColModalFooter]);

        // Or register all components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-drawer active position="right" size="small">
          <p>Drawer content goes here</p>
        </col-drawer>
        ```

    ### Best Practices

    * **Clear purpose**: Use drawers for secondary actions or content that doesn't require a full page navigation.
    * **Appropriate sizing**: Choose the drawer size based on content needs - small for navigation, medium for forms, large for detailed content.
    * **Focus management**: The drawer automatically traps focus when open, ensuring keyboard navigation stays within the drawer.
    * **Escape hatch**: Always provide a clear way to close the drawer (close button, backdrop click, or Escape key).
    * **Responsive design**: Consider how drawers behave on mobile devices - they may need to be full-width on smaller screens.
    * **Animation**: The drawer includes smooth slide-in/out animations for better user experience.

    ## When to use it

    * **Navigation menus**: Mobile-style navigation that slides in from the side
    * **Forms and filters**: Edit forms or filter panels that don't require full page navigation
    * **Settings panels**: Configuration or preference screens
    * **Shopping carts**: E-commerce cart summaries and checkout flows
    * **Detail views**: Additional information that supplements the main content
    * **Task panels**: Multi-step workflows or wizards

    ## Component preview
    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    ### Basic Drawer
    A simple drawer that slides in from the right side (default).
    <Canvas of={ColDrawerStories.Default} />

    ### Left Position
    Drawer sliding in from the left side of the viewport.
    <Canvas of={ColDrawerStories.LeftPosition} />

    ### Different Sizes
    The drawer component offers three preset sizes:

    #### Medium Size (640px)
    <Canvas of={ColDrawerStories.MediumSize} />

    #### Large Size (960px)
    <Canvas of={ColDrawerStories.LargeSize} />

    #### Custom Width
    You can also set a custom width in pixels.
    <Canvas of={ColDrawerStories.CustomWidth} />

    ### With Header and Footer
    Using modal header and footer components for consistent layout.
    <Canvas of={ColDrawerStories.WithHeaderFooter} />

    ### Navigation Drawer
    Example of a navigation menu drawer pattern.
    <Canvas of={ColDrawerStories.NavigationDrawer} />

    ### Form Drawer
    Example of using a drawer for form editing.
    <Canvas of={ColDrawerStories.FormDrawer} />

    ## Properties
    <ArgTypes of={ColDrawerStories} />

    ## Events

    | Event Name | Description | Event Detail |
    |------------|-------------|--------------|
    | `overlay-click-outside` | Fired when clicking outside the drawer (on the backdrop) | `{ overlay: ColOverlay, originalEvent: Event }` |
    | `overlay-escape` | Fired when pressing the Escape key | `{ overlay: ColOverlay, originalEvent: KeyboardEvent }` |

    ## Accessibility

    The Drawer component is built with accessibility in mind:

    * **Focus trap**: When open, focus is trapped within the drawer to prevent keyboard navigation outside
    * **ARIA attributes**: Proper ARIA labels and roles for screen readers
    * **Keyboard navigation**:
      - `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` to navigate through focusable elements
      - `Escape` to close the drawer (when not persistent)
    * **Backdrop click**: Clicking outside the drawer closes it (configurable)
    * **Scroll lock**: Body scroll is disabled when drawer is open to prevent background scrolling

    ### Disabling Focus Trap

    In some cases, you may want to disable the focus trap:
    ```html
    <col-drawer nofocustrap>
      <!-- Content -->
    </col-drawer>
    ```

    ## CSS Custom Properties

    You can customize the drawer width using CSS custom properties:

    ```css
    col-drawer {
      --col-drawer-width: 500px; /* Custom width */
    }
    ```

    ## JavaScript API

    ### Opening and Closing Programmatically

    ```javascript
    // Get drawer element
    const drawer = document.querySelector('col-drawer');

    // Open drawer
    drawer.active = true;
    // or
    drawer.setAttribute('active', 'true');

    // Close drawer
    drawer.active = false;
    // or
    drawer.removeAttribute('active');

    // Listen for close events
    drawer.addEventListener('overlay-click-outside', (e) => {
      console.log('Drawer closed by clicking outside');
    });
    ```

    ### Updating Focus Trap

    If you dynamically change the drawer content, you may need to update the focus trap:

    ```javascript
    const drawer = document.querySelector('col-drawer');

    // After adding new focusable elements
    drawer.updateFocusTrap();
    ```

    <Subtitle>Interact with the component</Subtitle>
    To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={ColDrawerStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.

  </div>
</div>



================================================
FILE: src/components/col-drawer/col-drawer.stories.tsx
================================================
import { css, html } from 'lit';
import type { ColibriStoryMeta, ColibriStory } from '@/types/storybook';
import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils/formatters';
import { fn } from '@storybook/test';

type StoryArgs = {
  active: boolean;
  position: 'left' | 'right';
  size: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large';
  width?: number;
  nofocustrap: boolean;
  headerContent?: string;
  footerContent?: string;
  mainContent?: string;
  onOverlayClickOutside?: () => void;
};

const meta = {
  title: 'Overlays/Drawer',
  component: 'col-drawer',
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        excludeDecorators: true,
        transform: formatCodeString,
      },
    },
    __sb: {
      minHeight: '400px',
    },
  },
  argTypes: {
    active: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Controls whether the drawer is visible',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
    },
    position: {
      control: 'select',
      options: ['left', 'right'],
      description: 'The edge from which the drawer slides',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'right' },
      },
    },
    size: {
      control: 'select',
      options: ['small', 'medium', 'large'],
      description: 'Preset drawer width (small: 448px, medium: 640px, large: 960px)',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'small' },
      },
    },
    width: {
      control: 'number',
      description: 'Custom width in pixels (overrides size preset)',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'number' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'undefined' },
      },
    },
    nofocustrap: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Disable focus trapping within the drawer',
      table: {
        category: 'Accessibility',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
    },
    headerContent: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'Content for the drawer header slot',
      table: {
        category: 'Slots',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
      },
    },
    footerContent: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'Content for the drawer footer slot',
      table: {
        category: 'Slots',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
      },
    },
    mainContent: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'Main content of the drawer',
      table: {
        category: 'Slots',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
      },
    },
    onOverlayClickOutside: {
      action: 'overlay-click-outside',
      description: 'Event fired when clicking outside the drawer',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
      },
    },
  },
  args: {
    active: false,
    position: 'right',
    size: 'small',
    nofocustrap: false,
    headerContent: '',
    footerContent: '',
    mainContent: 'This is the drawer content. Click outside or press Escape to close.',
    onOverlayClickOutside: fn(),
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<StoryArgs>;

export default meta;

type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

/**
 * Basic drawer render function
 */
const renderDrawer: Story['render'] = ({
  active,
  position,
  size,
  width,
  nofocustrap,
  headerContent,
  footerContent,
  mainContent,
  onOverlayClickOutside,
}) => {
  const ID = `drawer-${new Date().getTime()}`;
  const handleButtonClick = () => {
    const drawer = document.getElementById(ID);
    if (drawer) {
      drawer.setAttribute('active', 'true');
    }
  };

  return html`
    <div>
      <col-button color="primary" @click=${handleButtonClick}>Open Drawer</col-button>

      <col-drawer
        id=${ID}
        ?active=${active}
        position=${position}
        size=${size}
        width=${width || null}
        ?nofocustrap=${nofocustrap}
        @overlay-click-outside=${onOverlayClickOutside}
      >
        ${headerContent ? html` <div slot="header">${headerContent}</div> ` : ''} ${mainContent}
        ${footerContent ? html` <div slot="footer">${footerContent}</div> ` : ''}
      </col-drawer>
    </div>
  `;
};

/**
 * Default story showing a basic drawer
 */
export const Default: Story = {
  args: {
    active: false,
    position: 'right',
    size: 'small',
    mainContent: 'This is the drawer content. Click outside or press Escape to close.',
  },
  render: renderDrawer,
};

    size: 'small',
    mainContent: 'This drawer slides in from the left side of the screen.',
  },
  render: renderDrawer,
};

/**
 * Drawer with header and footer
 */
export const WithHeaderFooter: Story = {
  args: {
    active: false,
    position: 'right',
    size: 'small',
    headerContent: '',
    footerContent: '',
    mainContent: '',
  },
  render: ({ active, position, size, width, nofocustrap, onOverlayClickOutside }) => {
    const handleButtonClick = () => {
      const drawer = document.getElementById('drawer-header-footer');
      if (drawer) {
        drawer.setAttribute('active', 'true');
      }
    };

    const handleClose = () => {
      const drawer = document.getElementById('drawer-header-footer');
      if (drawer) {
        drawer.removeAttribute('active');
      }
    };

    return html`
      <div>
        <col-button color="primary" @click=${handleButtonClick}>
          Open Drawer with Header & Footer
        </col-button>

        <col-drawer
          id="drawer-header-footer"
          ?active=${active}
          position=${position}
          size=${size}
          width=${width || null}
          ?nofocustrap=${nofocustrap}
          @overlay-click-outside=${onOverlayClickOutside}
        >
          <col-modal-header slot="header" title="Drawer Title" @on-close=${handleClose}>
            <col-button slot="actions" aria-label="Full screen">
              <col-icon name="three-dots-vertical" size="16"></col-icon>
            </col-button>
          </col-modal-header>

          <div>
            <h3>Main Content Area</h3>
            <p>This drawer demonstrates the use of header and footer slots.</p>
            <p>The header includes a title and close button.</p>
            <p>The footer contains action buttons.</p>
          </div>

          <col-modal-footer slot="footer" description="This is helper text">
            <col-button slot="actions-left">Tertiary</col-button>
            <col-button slot="actions" @click=${handleClose}>Secondary</col-button>
            <col-button slot="actions" color="primary" @click=${handleClose}>Primary</col-button>
          </col-modal-footer>
        </col-drawer>
      </div>
    `;
  },
};

const navStylesExample = css`
  .navExample {
    padding: 20px;
  }

  .listExample {
    list-style: none;
    padding: 0;
    margin: 0;
  }

  .listItemExample {
    margin-bottom: 16px;
  }

  .itemExample {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 12px;
    text-decoration: none;
    color: inherit;
  }
`;

  args: {
    active: false,
    position: 'left',
    size: 'small',
  },
  render: ({ active, position, size, nofocustrap, onOverlayClickOutside }) => {
    const handleButtonClick = () => {
      const drawer = document.getElementById('drawer-navigation');
      if (drawer) {
        drawer.setAttribute('active', 'true');
      }
    };

    const handleClose = () => {
      const drawer = document.getElementById('drawer-navigation');
      if (drawer) {
        drawer.removeAttribute('active');
      }
    };

    return html`
      <style>
        ${navStylesExample}
      </style>
      <div>
        <col-button color="primary" @click=${handleButtonClick}>
          <col-icon name="six-dots"></col-icon>
          Open Navigation
        </col-button>

        <col-drawer
          id="drawer-navigation"
          ?active=${active}
          position=${position}
          size=${size}
          ?nofocustrap=${nofocustrap}
          @overlay-click-outside=${onOverlayClickOutside}
        >
          <col-modal-header
            slot="header"
            title="Navigation"
            @on-close=${handleClose}
          ></col-modal-header>

          <nav class="navExample">
            <ul class="listExample">
              <li class="listItemExample">
                <a href="#" class="itemExample">
                  <col-icon name="home"></col-icon>
                  <span>Home</span>
                </a>
              </li>

                  <col-icon name="person"></col-icon>
                  <span>Profile</span>
                </a>
              </li>
              <li class="listItemExample">
                <a href="#" class="itemExample">
                  <col-icon name="settings"></col-icon>
                  <span>Settings</span>
                </a>
              </li>
              <li class="listItemExample">

                  <col-icon name="alert-circle"></col-icon>
                  <span>Help</span>
                </a>

              Save Changes
            </col-button>
          </col-modal-footer>
        </col-drawer>
      </div>
    `;
  },
};

/**
 * Multiple drawers example
 */
export const MultipleDrawers: Story = {
  parameters: {
    __sb: {
      display: 'flex',
      gap: '16px',
    },
  },
  render: () => {
    const handleLeftClick = () => {
      const drawer = document.querySelector('#leftDrawer');
      if (drawer) {
        drawer.setAttribute('active', 'true');
      }
    };

    const handleRightClick = () => {
      const drawer = document.querySelector('#rightDrawer');
      if (drawer) {
        drawer.setAttribute('active', 'true');
      }
    };

    return html`
      <col-button @click=${handleLeftClick}>Open Left Drawer</col-button>
      <col-button @click=${handleRightClick}>Open Right Drawer</col-button>

      <col-drawer id="leftDrawer" position="left" size="small">
        <div>
          <h3>Left Drawer</h3>
          <p>This drawer slides in from the left.</p>
        </div>
      </col-drawer>

      <col-drawer id="rightDrawer" position="right" size="small">
        <div>
          <h3>Right Drawer</h3>
          <p>This drawer slides in from the right.</p>
        </div>
      </col-drawer>
    `;
  },
};

FILE: src/components/col-dropdown/col-dropdown.mdx
