import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import { Callout } from '@/_storybook/components';
import * as SelectStories from './col-select.stories';

<Meta of={SelectStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Select</Title>

    The Select component provides dropdown selection functionality with support for various states, validation, and two option rendering patterns: standard list menu items and custom data attributes.

    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
      <p>
        <strong>Single Selection Only</strong><br/>
        This component currently supports single-selection behavior only. Multi-select functionality will be available in a future version of the component library.
      </p>
    </Callout>

    ## Table of contents
    - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
    - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
    - [Component preview](#component-preview)
    - [Variants](#variants)
    - [States and Features](#states-and-features)
    - [Content and Layout](#content-and-layout)
    - [Option Types](#option-types)
    - [Form Integration](#form-integration)
    - [Accessibility](#accessibility)
    - [Properties](#properties)
    - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

    ## How to use it
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @colibri/colibri-components
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, registerAllComponents, ColSelect } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```

        <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
          **Dependency Note:** The `col-select` component depends on `col-typography`, `col-dropdown`, and `col-icon` components. These components are registered automatically as a dependency of the `col-select`component, so there's no need to import them.
        </Callout>

    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Option 1: Register specific component
        registerColibriComponents([ColSelect]);

        // Option 2: Register all Colibri components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-select
          id="user-preference"
          name="user-preference"
          label="Your Preference"
          placeholder="Choose an option"
        >
          <col-list-menu role="menuitem">
            <col-list-menu-item value="option1">Option 1</col-list-menu-item>
            <col-list-menu-item value="option2">Option 2</col-list-menu-item>
            <col-list-menu-item value="option3">Option 3</col-list-menu-item>
          </col-list-menu>
        </col-select>
        ```

    ## When to use it

    The `col-select` component is the standard choice for single-option dropdown selection. Use it when you need to:

    - **Collect Single Choices**: Gather selection data like categories, priorities, or preferences from a predefined list.
    - **Implement Form Dropdowns**: Integrate seamlessly into HTML forms for data submission and validation.
    - **Provide Loading States**: Display loading indicators while options are being fetched dynamically.
    - **Show Selection Feedback**: Use badges or counters to indicate selected items and selection count.
    - **Build Accessible Interfaces**: Ensure keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility for dropdown interactions.

    ## Component preview

    <div>
      The following examples showcase the core features and visual options of the `col-select` component.
    </div>
    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    ### Basic Usage
    <div>
      The most fundamental select requires only a `label` to identify its purpose and options to choose from.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.Default} />

    ## Variants

    <div>
      The `col-select` component supports two visual variants to accommodate different design contexts and user preferences.
    </div>

    ### Outline Variant
    <div>
      The outline variant displays a full border around the select field, providing clear visual boundaries. This is the default variant and is ideal for most form contexts where clear field separation is important.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.Variants} />

    ### Plain Variant
    <div>
      The plain variant removes the border entirely, creating a minimal appearance. The field only shows a border when focused or in error states, providing a cleaner, more modern look.
    </div>
    <Canvas className="col-doc__canvas--with-background" of={SelectStories.PlainVariant} />

    ## States and Features

    <div>
      The `col-select` component supports several states and features to reflect user interaction, loading states, and selection feedback.
    </div>

    ### Loading State
    <div>
      Use the `loading` property to display a spinner while options are being fetched or processed. This provides clear feedback to users during dynamic content loading.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.Loading} />

    ### With Badge
    <div>
      The `badge` property displays a small indicator showing the count of selected items. This is useful for providing visual feedback about the current selection state.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.WithBadge} />

    ### With Selection Counter
    <div>
      The `counter` property displays a selection counter in the format "X/Y" where X is the current selection and Y is the total available options.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.WithSelectionCounter} />

    ### With Badge and Counter
    <div>
      You can combine both `badge` and `counter` properties to provide comprehensive selection feedback with both visual indicators and numerical counts.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.WithBadgeAndCounter} />

    ### Disabled State
    <div>
      Use the `disabled` property to render the select non-interactive. This is useful for preventing user input when a field is not applicable or when the form is in a read-only state.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.Disabled} />

    ### Error State
    <div>
      The `error` property visually marks the select as invalid and displays an error message. This is typically set in response to failed validation or when a required field is empty.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.Error} />

    ### Required Field
    <div>
      Add the `required` property to ensure the field must have a selection before form submission. This triggers validation and prevents form submission when no option is selected.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.Required} />

    ### With Initial Value
    <div>
      You can provide an initial selected value using the `value` property. This is useful for displaying pre-selected options or restoring previous user selections.
    </div>
    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
      <p>
        <strong>Initial Value Behavior</strong><br/>
        The component automatically synchronizes the initial value with available options. If the provided value doesn't match any option, the selection will be cleared. If the matching option is disabled, the value will also be cleared to prevent invalid selections.
      </p>
    </Callout>

        <Canvas of={SelectStories.WithInitialValue} />

    ## Content and Layout

    <div>
      The `col-select` component provides flexible content and layout options to accommodate various design requirements and user interface patterns.
    </div>

    ### With Label
    <div>
      Use the `label` property to provide a clear, accessible label for the select field. The label is automatically associated with the input for screen readers and form integration.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.WithLabel} />

    ### With Sub Label
    <div>
      The `sub-label` property allows you to provide additional context or instructions below the main label. This is useful for complex forms where extra guidance is needed.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.WithSubLabel} />

    ### With Helper Text
    <div>
      Use the `helper` property to display helpful information below the select field. Helper text provides additional context without being part of the label structure.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.WithHelper} />

    ### With Icon
    <div>
      Add an icon to the select field using the `icon` slot. Icons can provide visual context and improve the overall user experience by making the field's purpose more apparent.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.WithIcon} />

    ## Option Types

    <div>
      The `col-select` component supports two different patterns for rendering options, each designed for specific use cases and styling requirements.
    </div>

    ### Standard List Menu Items
    <div>
      The first option uses `col-list-menu` and `col-list-menu-item` components to ensure styling aligns with the design system. This pattern provides consistent visual appearance and behavior across all select components.
    </div>
    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
      <p>
        <strong>Disabling Options</strong><br/>
        You can disable any option by setting the `disabled` boolean attribute to the `col-list-menu-item` component. It will automatically handle the disabled state and the visual appearance. See `Option 5` in the code snippet by clicking on the "Show code" button below.
      </p>
    </Callout>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.ColListMenuOptions} />

    ### Custom Data Attributes
    <div>
      The second option uses any HTML elements with `data-value` attributes. This approach allows complete control over the visual appearance while maintaining the component's functionality.
    </div>
    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
      <p>
        <strong>Disabling Options</strong><br/>
        You can disable any option by either setting the `disabled` boolean attribute or by adding `data-disabled="true"`. Disabled options cannot be selected and can be styled as needed. See `Custom Option 4` in the code snippet by clicking on the "Show code" button below.
      </p>
    </Callout>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.CustomDataOptions} />

    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      <p>
        <strong>Recommended Approach</strong><br/>
        Use `col-list-menu` and `col-list-menu-item` components for built-in features like keyboard navigation, selection states, link handling, and consistent theming. Custom data attributes should only be used for unique styling requirements.
      </p>
    </Callout>

    ## Form Integration

    <div>
      The `col-select` component integrates seamlessly with HTML forms, providing comprehensive validation, accessibility, and event handling for building robust user interfaces.
    </div>

    ### Custom Validation Message
    <div>
      You can provide custom validation messages using the `error-message` property to give users specific feedback about validation failures.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.WithCustomValidationMessage} />

    ### Interactive Form Example
    <div>
      This example demonstrates how the `col-select` component works within a real HTML form, including validation, form submission, and data handling.

      This preview shows the form structure and styling excluding the form submission and reset functionality. For the **full interactive experience** with working form submission, reset, and validation, visit the <LinkTo title={SelectStories.default.title} story="interactive-form-example">Canvas tab</LinkTo>.
    </Callout>
    <Canvas of={SelectStories.InteractiveFormExample} />

    ## Accessibility

    <div>
      The `col-select` component follows the [ARIA combobox pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/) and includes comprehensive accessibility features to ensure all users can interact with the component effectively.
    </div>

    ### Keyboard Navigation
    <div>
      The component supports full keyboard navigation for accessibility:
    </div>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Arrow Keys</strong>: Navigate through available options</li>
      <li><strong>Enter/Space</strong>: Select the currently focused option</li>
      <li><strong>Escape</strong>: Close the dropdown without making a selection</li>
      <li><strong>Tab</strong>: Move focus to the next form element</li>
    </ul>

    ### Screen Reader Support
    <div>
      The component includes proper ARIA attributes and live regions for screen reader compatibility:
    </div>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>ARIA Labels</strong>: Proper labeling for form integration</li>
      <li><strong>Live Regions</strong>: Announce state changes and validation messages</li>
      <li><strong>Role Attributes</strong>: Correct semantic roles for combobox pattern</li>
      <li><strong>State Announcements</strong>: Clear feedback for selection changes</li>
    </ul>

    ### Focus Management
    <div>
      The component implements proper focus management to ensure a logical tab order and return focus appropriately after interactions.
    </div>

    ## Properties

    <div>
      The `col-select` component provides a comprehensive set of properties for customization, validation, and integration with forms and applications.
    </div>
    <ArgTypes of={SelectStories} />

    ## Interact with the component
    To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={SelectStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.

  </div>
</div>

export default meta;
type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

const renderColSelect: Story['render'] = args => {
  const renderOptions = () => {
    if (!args.showOptions) return nothing;

    if (args.optionType === 'col-list-menu') {
      return html`
        <col-list-menu role="menuitem">
          <col-list-menu-item value="option1">Option 1</col-list-menu-item>
          <col-list-menu-item value="option2">Option 2</col-list-menu-item>
          <col-list-menu-item value="option3">Option 3</col-list-menu-item>
          <col-list-menu-item value="option4">Option 4</col-list-menu-item>
          <col-list-menu-item value="option5" disabled>Option 5 (Disabled)</col-list-menu-item>
        </col-list-menu>
      `;
    }

    return html`
      <div class="custom-options" role="menuitem">
        <div data-value="custom1">Custom Option 1</div>
        <div data-value="custom2">Custom Option 2</div>
        <div data-value="custom3">Custom Option 3</div>
        <div data-value="custom4" data-disabled="true">Custom Option 4 (Disabled)</div>
      </div>
    `;
  };

  return html`
    <col-select
      id=${args.id || nothing}
      name=${args.name || nothing}
      value=${args.value || nothing}
      label=${args.label || nothing}
      sub-label=${args.subLabel || nothing}
      helper=${args.helper || nothing}
      placeholder=${args.placeholder || nothing}
      variant=${args.variant}
      placement=${args.placement}
      error-message=${args.errorMessage || nothing}
      custom-width=${args.customWidth || nothing}
      ?clearable=${args.clearable}
      ?loading=${args.loading}
      ?disabled=${args.disabled}
      ?error=${args.error}
      ?required=${args.required}
      ?badge=${args.badge}
      ?counter=${args.counter}
      @input=${args.input}
      @change=${args.change}
      @focus=${args.focus}
      @blur=${args.blur}
      @select-option-selected=${args.selectOptionSelected}
      @select-cleared=${args.selectCleared}
      @select-opened=${args.selectOpened}
      @select-closed=${args.selectClosed}
      @invalid=${args.invalid}
    >
      ${args.iconVisible
        ? html`<col-icon slot="icon" name=${args.iconName} size=${args.iconSize}></col-icon>`
        : nothing}
      ${renderOptions()}
    </col-select>
  `;
};

export const InteractiveFormExample: Story = {
  name: 'Interactive Form Example',
  parameters: {
    controls: { disable: true },
    __sb: { height: '100%' },
    docs: {
      source: {
        transform: (code: string) => {
          const formMatch = code.match(/<form[^>]*slot="form"[^>]*>[\s\S]*?<\/form>/);
          return formatCodeString(formMatch?.[0] || '');
        },
      },
    },
  },
  render: () => {
    const formId = 'select-form-example';
    const outputId = 'select-form-output';
    const isInDocs = window.location.search.includes('viewMode=docs');
    const codeSnippet = hljs.highlightAuto(`
      // Handle form submit with FormValidationController
      form.addEventListener('submit', (event) => {
        // Check if validation was already prevented
        if (event.defaultPrevented) {
          console.log('Form submission blocked by validation');
          return;
        }

        // Prevent page reload
        event.preventDefault();

        // Process form data only if validation passed
        const formData = new FormData(form);
        const formValues = Object.fromEntries(formData.entries());
        console.log('Form submitted successfully:', formValues);
      });

      // Handle form reset - FormResetController handles component reset automatically
      form.addEventListener('reset', () => {
        // Only handle UI cleanup - components reset automatically
        // A custom message or action can be added here
        console.log('Form reset completed');
      });
    `).value;

    return html`
      <form-demo
        form-id="${formId}"
        output-id="${outputId}"
        title="User Preferences Form"
        code-snippet="${codeSnippet}"
        code-theme="dark"
      >
        <form slot="form" id="${formId}" class="form-container">
          <col-select
            id="country"
            name="country"
            label="Country"
            value="us"
            placeholder="Select your country"
            required
            helper="This will be used for shipping."
            error-message="Please select a country"
            custom-width="100%"
          >
            <col-list-menu role="menuitem">
              <col-list-menu-item value="us">United States</col-list-menu-item>
              <col-list-menu-item value="ca">Canada</col-list-menu-item>
              <col-list-menu-item value="uk">United Kingdom</col-list-menu-item>
              <col-list-menu-item value="de">Germany</col-list-menu-item>
              <col-list-menu-item value="fr">France</col-list-menu-item>
            </col-list-menu>
          </col-select>
          <col-select
            id="role"
            name="role"
            label="User Role"
            value="admin"
            placeholder="Select your role"
            required
            badge
            counter
            helper="This determines your access level."
            error-message="Please select a role"
            custom-width="100%"
          >
            <col-list-menu role="menuitem">
              <col-list-menu-item value="admin">Administrator</col-list-menu-item>
              <col-list-menu-item value="user">Standard User</col-list-menu-item>
              <col-list-menu-item value="guest">Guest</col-list-menu-item>
            </col-list-menu>
          </col-select>
          <col-select
            id="theme"
            name="theme"
            label="Theme Preference"
            value="light"
            helper="Choose your preferred theme."
            custom-width="100%"
          >
            <col-list-menu role="menuitem">
              <col-list-menu-item value="light">Light Theme</col-list-menu-item>
              <col-list-menu-item value="dark">Dark Theme</col-list-menu-item>
              <col-list-menu-item value="auto">Auto (System)</col-list-menu-item>
            </col-list-menu>
          </col-select>
          <col-group>
            <col-button type="submit" ?disabled=${isInDocs}>Submit</col-button>
            <col-button type="reset" ?disabled=${isInDocs}>Reset</col-button>
          </col-group>
        </form>
      </form-demo>
    `;
  },
};

FILE: src/components/col-site-menu/col-site-menu.mdx

import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Title, Subtitle, Source, Unstyled } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import { Callout, Container } from '@/_storybook/components';
import * as ColSiteMenuStories from './col-site-menu.stories';

<Meta of={ColSiteMenuStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Site Menu</Title>
    A comprehensive navigation organism that orchestrates toolbar and submenu interactions for complex application navigation.
    The Site Menu provides context-driven state management, single-open accordion behavior, and flexible slot-based composition.
    This organism component manages all child component state through Lit Context and enforces single-open accordion behavior for subcategories.

    <Subtitle>Table of Contents</Subtitle>
    - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
    - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
    - [Component preview](#component-preview)
    - [Child Components](#child-components)
      - [col-site-menu-toolbar](#col-site-menu-toolbar)
      - [col-site-menu-button](#col-site-menu-button)
      - [col-site-menu-submenu](#col-site-menu-submenu)
      - [col-site-menu-category](#col-site-menu-category)
      - [col-site-menu-subcategory](#col-site-menu-subcategory)
      - [col-site-menu-item](#col-site-menu-item)
    - [Advanced Features](#advanced-features)
    - [Properties](#properties)
    - [Events](#events)
    - [Accessibility](#accessibility)
    - [Context architecture](#context-architecture)
    - [Dynamic Content Integration](#dynamic-content-integration)
    - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

    ## How to use it
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        // Option 1: For registering specific components
        import {
          registerColibriComponents,
          ColSiteMenu,
          ColSiteMenuSubmenu,
          ColSiteMenuToolbar,
        } from '@telesign/colibri';

        // Option 2: For registering all Colibri components
        import { registerAllComponents } from '@telesign/colibri';
        ```
    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Option 1: Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColSiteMenu, ColSiteMenuSubmenu, ColSiteMenuToolbar])

        // Option 2: Register all Colibri components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```

    4. Use it in your HTML:
          <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
            **Dependency Note**

            When registering specific components, you only need to register `ColSiteMenu`, `ColSiteMenuSubmenu`, and `ColSiteMenuToolbar`.
            All other Site Menu components are registered automatically as dependencies.
          </Callout>
        ```html
        <col-site-menu color="primary">
          <col-site-menu-toolbar slot="toolbar">
            <col-icon slot="logo" name="home" size="24"></col-icon>
            <col-site-menu-button slot="actions" action-id="dashboard">
              <col-icon name="dash-board" size="20"></col-icon>
            </col-site-menu-button>
          </col-site-menu-toolbar>
          <col-site-menu-submenu slot="submenu">
            <col-site-menu-category category-name="dashboard">
              <col-icon name="stats" size="20"></col-icon>
              Dashboard
            </col-site-menu-category>
            <col-site-menu-subcategory subcategory-id="overview">
              <col-icon slot="icon" name="sight-on" size="18"></col-icon>
              Overview
              <col-site-menu-item slot="items" href="/dashboard/home" router>
                Home
              </col-site-menu-item>
            </col-site-menu-subcategory>
          </col-site-menu-submenu>
        </col-site-menu>
        ```
    ## When to use it
    Use the Site Menu organism when you need sophisticated navigation capabilities that go beyond simple menu components.
    This component is designed for complex applications that require multi-level navigation with coordinated state management.

    **Ideal Use Cases:**
    - **Complex Navigation** - Multi-level navigation with categories, subcategories, and items
    - **Context-Driven State** - Centralized state management across multiple child components
    - **Single-Open Accordion** - Enforce single-open behavior for subcategories
    - **Flexible Composition** - Slot-based API for maximum customization
    - **Accessibility Features** - Proper ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and focus management

    ## Component preview

    The Site Menu provides two interactive demonstrations showcasing its navigation capabilities:

    ### Basic site menu

    The default configuration demonstrates core functionality with dashboard and analytics navigation, featuring multi-level content organization and single-open accordion behavior.

    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
     To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={ColSiteMenuStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.
    </Callout>

    ### Interactive example

    Explore all configuration options including color variants, state management, and responsive behavior. Use the controls panel to test different combinations.

    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
    To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={ColSiteMenuStories.default.title} story="interactive-example">Interactive Example</LinkTo> section.
    </Callout>

    ## Child Components
    The Site Menu includes six child components that work together through shared context to provide complete navigation functionality. Each component has a specific role in building the overall navigation experience.

    We'll describe each component following a top-down approach, starting with the main container components and moving to the individual navigation elements. For detailed implementation examples, see the HTML code snippet in the [How to use it](#how-to-use-it) section.

    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
      The following component examples are displayed individually for documentation purposes and are not interactive.
      They demonstrate the structure and appearance of each child component within the Site Menu ecosystem.
    </Callout>

    ### `col-site-menu-toolbar`

    The primary navigation toolbar that serves as the header component for the Site Menu. It provides dedicated slots for branding elements (logo) and interactive action buttons that control submenu content. The toolbar acts as the main interaction point for users to switch between different navigation contexts and maintains visual consistency across the application.

    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.ToolbarExample} />

    #### Key Implementation Details

    **Required Configuration:**
    - **`slot="toolbar"`** - Must be placed in the Site Menu as the toolbar slot
    - **`slot="actions"`** - Each `col-site-menu-button` requires this slot within the toolbar (see [col-site-menu-button](#col-site-menu-button) section for details)

    **Essential Components:**
    - **General Slot** - Located at the top of the toolbar. Accepts any content (typically an `svg` file) for branding
    - **Action Buttons** - Uses `col-site-menu-button` components for navigation triggers
    - **Context Integration** - Automatically receives color themes and manages selection state

    **Accessibility features:**
    - **`aria-label`** - Required on both toolbar and each button for screen reader support
    - **Semantic Structure** - Toolbar uses proper navigation landmarks
    - **Keyboard Navigation** - Full tab and arrow key support for all interactive elements

    ### `col-site-menu-button`

    Individual action buttons within the toolbar that trigger submenu content changes. These buttons are context-aware and automatically synchronize their selection state with the Site Menu's current navigation context. Each button represents a distinct navigation section and communicates with the parent Site Menu through the shared context system.

    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    #### Enabled State

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.ButtonExampleEnabled} />

    #### Selected State

    <Canvas className='col-doc__canvas--with-background' of={ColSiteMenuStories.ButtonExampleSelected} />


    #### Disabled State

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.ButtonExampleDisabled} />

    #### With Tooltip

    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
      The `label` property provides an optional way to display button labels as tooltips when the site menu is collapsed. When provided, the button is automatically wrapped with a `col-tooltip` that appears on hover. For more details on tooltip configuration, see the [Tooltip documentation](./?path=/docs/overlays-tooltip--overview).
    </Callout>

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.ButtonExampleWithTooltip} />


    #### Key Implementation Details
    **Required Configuration:**
    - **`action-id`** - Unique identifier to link the button to submenu content

    **Essential Components:**
    - **General Slot** - Typically contains a `col-icon` for visual representation
    - **Context Integration** - Automatically updates selection state based on Site Menu context

    **Tooltip Integration:**
    - **Optional Labels** - Use the `label` property to display tooltips when collapsed
    - **Automatic Wrapping** - Buttons with labels are wrapped in `col-tooltip` components
    - **Context-Aware** - Tooltips are disabled when the site menu is open
    - **Seamless Experience** - Provides helpful context without interfering with navigation


    **Accessibility features:**
    - **`aria-label`** - Descriptive label for screen readers. Defaults to "Navigation action" if not provided, or uses the `label` property value if `aria-label` is not explicitly set
    - **`aria-pressed`** - Automatically managed to reflect selection state
    - **Keyboard Focus** - Fully focusable and operable via keyboard inputs

    ### `col-site-menu-submenu`

    Container for submenu content that appears when an action is selected. This component serves as the content area that displays navigation categories, subcategories, and items based on the currently selected toolbar action. The submenu automatically manages its visibility through the Site Menu context and provides a structured layout for organizing complex navigation hierarchies.

    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.SubmenuExample} />

    #### Key Implementation Details

    **Required Configuration:**
    - **`slot="submenu"`** - Must be placed in the Site Menu as the submenu slot
    - **Proper nesting structure** - Contains `col-site-menu-category`, `col-site-menu-subcategory`, and `col-site-menu-item` components

    **Essential Components:**
    - **Category Components** - Header sections that group related navigation content
    - **Subcategory Components** - Collapsible sections containing menu items (only one can be opened at a time)
    - **Menu Item Components** - Individual navigation links and actions
    - **Context Integration** - Automatically receives visibility state and color themes

    **Accessibility features:**
    - **`aria-label`** - Descriptive label for screen readers. Defaults to "Site navigation submenu" if not provided
    - **`aria-hidden`** - Automatically managed based on visibility state
    - **`inert` attribute** - Applied when hidden to prevent interaction with hidden content
    - **Focus management** - Automatically focuses appropriate elements when opened

    **Keyboard Navigation:**
    - **Escape key** - Closes open subcategories first, then closes the entire submenu
    - **Automatic focus** - Focuses first category or focusable element when submenu opens
    - **Tab navigation** - Standard tab order through all interactive elements

    ### `col-site-menu-category`
    The category header component that organizes related subcategories within the submenu. Each category serves as a visual and semantic grouping for navigation items, providing clear separation between different sections of the menu. Categories enhance the overall structure and usability of the navigation experience.

    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippet to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.CategoryExample} />

    #### Key Implementation Details
    **Required Configuration:**
    - **`category-name`** - Unique identifier for the category (used for event tracking via the `site-menu-category-close` event)
    - **Proper nesting structure** - Should contain `col-icon` and text content

    **Essential Components:**
    - **General Slot** - Typically contains a `col-icon` for visual representation and text for the category name
    - **Semantic Structure** - Renders as a heading element for proper document structure
    - **Context Integration** - Receives color themes from Site Menu context

    **Accessibility features:**
    - **`aria-label`** - Descriptive label for screen readers.
    - **Focus management** - Automatically focuses first category or focusable element when submenu opens

    **Keyboard Navigation**
    - Fully focusable and operable via keyboard inputs

    ### `col-site-menu-subcategory`
    The subcategory header component that provides collapsible sections within a category. Each subcategory can be expanded or collapsed to show or hide its associated menu items. The component enforces single-open accordion behavior, ensuring that only one subcategory can be open at a time. This enhances usability by reducing visual clutter and focusing user attention.

    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippet to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    #### Default (Closed) State

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.SubcategoryExampleDefault} />

    #### Open State

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.SubcategoryExampleOpen} />

    ### Disabled State

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.SubcategoryExampleDisabled} />

    #### Key Implementation Details
    **Required Configuration:**
    - **`subcategory-id`** - Unique identifier to manage open/close state
    - **`open` attribute** - Optional boolean to set initial open state (default is closed)
    - **`disabled` attribute** - Optional boolean to disable interaction
    - **Proper nesting structure** - Should contain `col-icon`, text content, and `col-site-menu-item` components

    **Essential Components:**
    - **Icon Slot** - Dedicated slot for the icon element
    - **General Slot** - Typically contains the text for the subcategory name
    - **Item Container Slot** - Contains `col-site-menu-item` components
    - **Context Integration** - Manages open/close state through Site Menu context

    **Accessibility features:**
    - **`aria-label`** - Descriptive label for screen readers. Default to "Toggle subcategory" if not provided
    - **`aria-expanded`** - Automatically managed to reflect open/close state
    - **`role="button"`** - Applied to the header for proper semantics

    **Focus management**
    - Focuses header when toggled and manages focus within item container

    **Keyboard Navigation:**
    - **Enter/Space** - Toggles open/close state
    - **Tab key** - Navigate within item container when open. **No arrow key navigation in the current implementation**.
    - **Escape key** - Closes subcategory and returns focus to header

    ### `col-site-menu-item`
    The individual navigation item component that represents a single link or action within a subcategory. Each item can function as a router link, external link, or action button. The component is designed to be flexible and accessible, providing clear navigation options for users.

    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    #### Default State

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.ItemExample} />

    ### Selected State

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.ItemExampleSelected} />

    ### Disabled State

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.ItemExampleDisabled} />

    ### Router Navigation
    This example demonstrates a router link item that integrates with client-side routing libraries. It uses the `router` attribute to indicate that it should be treated as a router link. The `href` attribute specifies the target route. The item automatically manages its selected state based on the current route.

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.RouterItemExample} />

    ### External Links
    This example demonstrates an external link item that opens a new browser tab. It uses the `href` attribute to specify the URL and the `target="_blank"` attribute to open the link in a new tab. The `rel="noopener noreferrer"` attribute is included for security best practices.

    <Canvas of={ColSiteMenuStories.ExternalItemExample} />

    #### Key Implementation Details
    **Required Configuration:**
    - **`item-name`** - Unique identifier for the item (used for event tracking via the `site-menu-item-selected` event)
    - **`parent-subcategory-id`** - Identifier of the parent subcategory (used for single-open behavior coordination and focus management. Must match the `subcategory-id` of the parent subcategory).
    - **`href` attribute** - URL or route for navigation
    - **`slot="items"`** - Must be placed in a `col-site-menu-subcategory` as an item slot
    - **Optional `router` attribute** - Indicates a router link (integrates with client-side routing libraries)
    - **Optional `target` attribute** - Specifies where to open the link (e.g., `_blank` for new tab)
    - **Optional `rel` attribute** - Security attributes for external links (e.g., `noopener noreferrer`)
    - **Proper nesting structure** - Should contain text content and optionally a `col-icon`

    **Essential Components:**
    - **General Slot** - Typically contains text content
    - **Context Integration** - Automatically manages selected state based on current route (for router links)
    - **Link Behavior** - Renders as an anchor tag for navigation

    **Accessibility features:**
    - **`aria-label`** - Descriptive label for screen readers. Defaults to link
    - **`role="link"`** - Applied for proper semantics
    - **Focus management** - Fully focusable and operable via keyboard inputs

    **Keyboard Navigation:**
    - **Enter/Space** - Activates the link
    - **Tab key** - Navigate through all interactive elements
    - **Standard link behavior** - Follows platform conventions for links

    ## Advanced Features

    ### Keyboard Navigation

    The Site Menu provides comprehensive keyboard navigation support:

    - **Tab Navigation**: Navigate between interactive elements
    - **Enter/Space**: Activate buttons and toggle subcategories
    - **Escape**: Close submenu and return focus to toolbar

    ### Responsive Behavior

    The Site Menu adapts to different screen sizes:

    - **Desktop**: Full sidebar layout with persistent toolbar
    - **Mobile**: Overlay behavior with optimized touch targets
    - **Flexible Width**: Configurable toolbar and submenu widths

    ### Focus Management

    Sophisticated focus management ensures accessibility:

    - **Focus Restoration**: Returns focus to toolbar when submenu closes
    - **Focus Trap**: Manages focus within open subcategories
    - **Visible Focus Indicators**: Clear focus states for all interactive elements

    ### Single-Open Accordion

    Enforces single-open behavior for subcategories:

    - **Automatic Closing**: Opening one subcategory closes others
    - **Context Coordination**: State managed through Site Menu context
    - **Event Propagation**: Proper event handling for state changes

    ## Properties

    ### `col-site-menu` properties

    <ArgTypes of={ColSiteMenuStories} include="^(color|selected-action-id|open|open-subcategory-id|aria-label)$" />

    ### `col-site-menu-toolbar` properties

    <ArgTypes of={ColSiteMenuStories} include="^(aria-label)$" />

    ### `col-site-menu-button` properties

    <ArgTypes of={ColSiteMenuStories} include="^(action-id|selected|aria-label|label|disabled)$" />

    ### `col-site-menu-submenu` properties

    <ArgTypes of={ColSiteMenuStories} include="^(aria-label)$" />

    <ArgTypes of={ColSiteMenuStories} include="^(category-name|aria-label)$" />

    ### `col-site-menu-subcategory` properties

    <ArgTypes of={ColSiteMenuStories} include="^(subcategory-id|open|disabled|aria-label)$" />

    ### `col-site-menu-item` properties

    <ArgTypes of={ColSiteMenuStories} include="^(item-name|parent-subcategory-id|href|router|aria-label|disabled|selected)$" />

    ## Events

    The Site Menu emits several custom events for integration:

    ### `site-menu-action-selected`

    Fired when a toolbar action is selected.

    ```typescript
    interface SiteMenuActionSelectedDetail {
      actionId: string;
      selected: boolean;
    }
    ```

    ### `site-menu-submenu-toggle`

    Fired when submenu visibility changes.

    ```typescript
    interface SiteMenuSubmenuToggleDetail {
      visible: boolean;
      actionId?: string;
    }
    ```

    ### `site-menu-subcategory-toggle`

    Fired when subcategory open/close state changes.

    ```typescript
    interface SiteMenuSubcategoryToggleDetail {
      subcategoryId: string;
      open: boolean;
      openSubcategoryId?: string;
    }
    ```

    ## Accessibility

    The Site Menu is designed with accessibility as a core principle:

    ### ARIA Support

    - **Navigation Role**: Main container uses `role="navigation"`
    - **Button Semantics**: Proper button roles and states
    - **Expanded States**: `aria-expanded` for collapsible elements
    - **Labeling**: Comprehensive `aria-label` attributes

    ### Screen Reader Support

    - **Semantic Structure**: Proper heading hierarchy
    - **State Announcements**: Clear state changes
    - **Content Relationships**: Parent-child relationships

    ### Keyboard Accessibility

    - **Full Keyboard Navigation**: All functionality accessible via keyboard
    - **Focus Management**: Logical focus order and restoration
    - **Standard Shortcuts**: Follows platform conventions

    ## Context Architecture

    ### Context-Driven State Management

    The Site Menu uses Lit Context for centralized state management:

    ```typescript
    interface SiteMenuContextValue {
      color: SITE_MENU_COLOR_VARIANTS;
      selectedActionId?: string;
      open: boolean;
      openSubcategoryId?: string;
      // Context methods for state manipulation
    }
    ```

    ### Single-Open Enforcement

    The context ensures only one subcategory can be open at a time, providing a consistent accordion experience across the entire navigation structure.

    ## Dynamic Content Integration

    The Site Menu supports dynamic content switching through JavaScript event handlers. This is particularly useful for applications that need to show different submenu content based on user selections.

    ### Event-Driven Content Updates

    When a toolbar action is selected, you can listen for the `site-menu-action-selected` event and dynamically update the submenu content:

    ```javascript
    const handleActionSelected = (e) => {
      const { actionId } = e.detail;
      const submenuElement = e.target?.querySelector('col-site-menu-submenu');

      if (submenuElement) {
        submenuElement.innerHTML = '';

        if (actionId === 'dashboard') {
          submenuElement.innerHTML = `
            <col-site-menu-category category-name="dashboard">
              <col-icon name="dash-board" size="20"></col-icon>
              Dashboard
            </col-site-menu-category>
            <!-- Add subcategories and items here -->
          `;
        }
      }
    };

    document.querySelector('col-site-menu')
      ?.addEventListener('site-menu-action-selected', handleActionSelected);
    ```

    ### Integration with Frameworks

    For framework integration (React, Vue, Angular), you can manage the submenu content through component state:

    **React Example:**
    ```jsx
    const [submenuContent, setSubmenuContent] = useState('');

    const handleActionSelected = (e) => {
      const { actionId } = e.detail;

      if (actionId === 'dashboard') {
        setSubmenuContent(dashboardMenuHTML);
      } else if (actionId === 'analytics') {
        setSubmenuContent(analyticsMenuHTML);
      }
    };

    return (
      <col-site-menu onSite-menu-action-selected={handleActionSelected}>
        <col-site-menu-submenu
          slot="submenu"
          dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: submenuContent }}
        />
      </col-site-menu>
    );
    ```

    ### Best Practices

    - **Performance**: Cache submenu content templates to avoid repeated string generation
    - **Accessibility**: Ensure dynamic content maintains proper ARIA labels and structure
    - **State Management**: Use your application's state management system for complex navigation scenarios
    - **Loading States**: Consider showing loading indicators during content transitions

    ## Interact with the component

    To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={ColSiteMenuStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.
  </div>
</div>

import { html, nothing, LitElement, css } from 'lit';
import { customElement, property, state } from 'lit/decorators.js';

import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils/formatters';
import '@/_storybook/components/LayoutDemo';
import '@/_storybook/components/MainLogo';

type SiteMenuProps = {
  color: string;
  open: boolean;
  ariaLabel: string;
};

  disabled: boolean;
  ariaLabel: string;
  label: string;

  subcategoryId: string;
  itemName: string;
  href: string;
  newTab: boolean;
  router: boolean;
  parentSubcategoryId: string;
  selected: boolean;
};

    showColorControl: boolean;

    toggleSubmenu: () => void;
    toggleSubcategory: () => void;
    handleClick: () => void;
  };

    router: {
      control: { type: 'boolean' },
      description: 'Item: Whether to use SPA router navigation. Use `router` attribute in HTML.',
      table: {
        category: 'link',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'router', neq: false },
    },
    parentSubcategoryId: {
      name: 'parent-subcategory-id',
      control: { type: 'text' },
      description:
        'Item: ID of parent subcategory for accessibility. Use `parent-subcategory-id="overview"` in HTML.',
      table: {
        category: 'core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'undefined' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'parentSubcategoryId', neq: '' },
    },
    showColorControl: {
      control: { type: 'boolean' },
      description: 'Show color control (Storybook only)',
      table: {
        category: 'Storybook',
      },
      if: { arg: 'showColorControl', neq: false },
    },
    selectAction: {
      action: 'site-menu-action-selected',
      description: 'Fired when a toolbar action button is selected.',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
        type: { summary: '{ actionId: string }' },
      },
    },
    toggleSubmenu: {
      action: 'site-menu-submenu-toggle',
      description: 'Fired when the submenu panel is opened or closed.',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
        type: { summary: '{ open: boolean }' },
      },
    },
    toggleSubcategory: {
      action: 'site-menu-subcategory-toggle',
      description: 'Fired when a subcategory is expanded or collapsed.',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
        type: { summary: '{ subcategoryId: string; open: boolean }' },
      },
    },
    handleClick: {
      action: 'site-menu-item-click',
      description: 'Fired when a menu item is clicked.',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
        type: { summary: '{ itemName: string; href: string }' },
      },
    },
  },
  args: {
    color: 'primary',
    open: false,
    disabled: false,
    selected: false,
    ariaLabel: '',
    label: '',
    categoryName: '',
    subcategoryId: '',
    itemName: '',
    href: '',
    newTab: false,
    router: false,
    parentSubcategoryId: '',
    showColorControl: false,
    selectAction: action('site-menu-action-selected'),
    toggleSubmenu: action('site-menu-submenu-toggle'),
    toggleSubcategory: action('site-menu-subcategory-toggle'),
    handleClick: action('site-menu-item-click'),
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<SiteMenuStoryArgs>;

export default meta;

type Story = ColibriStory<SiteMenuStoryArgs>;

const CategoryTemplates = {
  dashboard: (categoryName?: string) => `
    <col-site-menu-category category-name="${categoryName || 'dashboard'}" aria-label="Close Dashboard">
      <col-icon name="dash-board" size="20"></col-icon>
      Dashboard
    </col-site-menu-category>
  `,

  analytics: (categoryName?: string) => `
    <col-site-menu-category category-name="${categoryName || 'analytics'}" aria-label="Close Analytics">
      <col-icon name="trending-up-square" size="20"></col-icon>
      Analytics
    </col-site-menu-category>
  `,
};

const SubcategoryTemplates = {
  overview: (
    options: {
      subcategoryId?: string;
      ariaLabel?: string;
      disabled?: boolean;
    } = {}
  ) => {
    const { subcategoryId = 'overview', ariaLabel = 'Toggle Overview', disabled = false } = options;

    return `
    <col-site-menu-subcategory subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" aria-label="${ariaLabel}" ${disabled ? 'disabled' : ''}>
      <col-icon slot="icon" name="sight-on" size="18"></col-icon>
      Overview
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="home" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" href="/dashboard/home" router>Home</col-site-menu-item>
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="activity" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" href="/dashboard/activity">Recent Activity</col-site-menu-item>
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="stats" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}">Quick Stats</col-site-menu-item>
    </col-site-menu-subcategory>
  `;
  },

  reports: (
    options: {
      subcategoryId?: string;
      ariaLabel?: string;
      disabled?: boolean;
    } = {}
  ) => {
    const { subcategoryId = 'reports', ariaLabel = 'Toggle Reports', disabled = false } = options;

    return `
      <col-site-menu-subcategory subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" aria-label="${ariaLabel}" ${disabled ? 'disabled' : ''}>
      <col-icon slot="icon" name="documentation" size="18"></col-icon>
      Reports
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="analytics" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" href="/dashboard/reports/analytics" router>Analytics</col-site-menu-item>
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="export" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" href="/dashboard/reports/export" router>Export Data</col-site-menu-item>
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="scheduled" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}">Scheduled Reports</col-site-menu-item>
    </col-site-menu-subcategory>
    `;
  },

  metrics: (
    options: {
      subcategoryId?: string;
      ariaLabel?: string;
      disabled?: boolean;
    } = {}
  ) => {
    const { subcategoryId, ariaLabel, disabled } = options;

    return `
    <col-site-menu-subcategory subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" aria-label="${ariaLabel || 'Toggle Metrics'}" ${disabled ? 'disabled' : ''}>
      <col-icon slot="icon" name="signal" size="18"></col-icon>
      Metrics
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="performance" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" href="/analytics/performance" router>Performance</col-site-menu-item>
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="traffic" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" href="/analytics/traffic" router>Traffic Analysis</col-site-menu-item>
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="conversion" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}">Conversion Rates</col-site-menu-item>
    </col-site-menu-subcategory>
  `;
  },

  insights: (
    options: {
      subcategoryId?: string;
      ariaLabel?: string;
      disabled?: boolean;
    } = {}
  ) => {
    const { subcategoryId = 'insights', ariaLabel = 'Toggle Insights', disabled = false } = options;

    return `
    <col-site-menu-subcategory subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" aria-label="${ariaLabel}" ${disabled ? 'disabled' : ''}>
      <col-icon slot="icon" name="monitoring" size="18"></col-icon>
      Insights
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="trends" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" href="/analytics/trends" router>Trends</col-site-menu-item>
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="forecasting" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}" href="/analytics/forecast" router>Forecasting</col-site-menu-item>
      <col-site-menu-item slot="items" item-name="recommendations" parent-subcategory-id="${subcategoryId}">Recommendations</col-site-menu-item>
    </col-site-menu-subcategory>
  `;
  },
};

export const SubmenuContentTemplates = {
  dashboardContent: () => `
    ${CategoryTemplates.dashboard()}
    ${SubcategoryTemplates.overview()}
    ${SubcategoryTemplates.reports()}
  `,

  analyticsContent: () => `
    ${CategoryTemplates.analytics()}
    ${SubcategoryTemplates.metrics()}
    ${SubcategoryTemplates.insights()}
  `,
};

const renderButton = (args: SiteMenuStoryArgs) => html`
  <col-site-menu-button
    slot="actions"
    action-id="dashboard"
    label="${ifDefined(args.label)}"
    aria-label="${args.ariaLabel}"
    ?disabled=${args.disabled}
    ?selected=${args.selected}
  >
    <col-icon name="dash-board" size="20"></col-icon>
  </col-site-menu-button>
`;

const renderItem = (args: SiteMenuStoryArgs) => html`
  <col-site-menu-item
    item-name="${args.itemName}"
    parent-subcategory-id="${args.parentSubcategoryId}"
    aria-label="${args.ariaLabel}"
    href="${args.href || nothing}"
    ?disabled=${args.disabled}
    ?selected=${args.selected}
    ?newtab=${args.newTab}
    ?router=${args.router}
  >
    ${args.itemName}
  </col-site-menu-item>
`;

const renderSiteMenuWithSwitching = (args: SiteMenuStoryArgs) => {
  return html`
    <site-menu-story-wrapper
      color="${args.color}"
      ?open=${args.open}
      aria-label="${args.ariaLabel}"
    ></site-menu-story-wrapper>
  `;
};

export const Default: Story = {
  parameters: {
    __sb: {
      height: '100dvh',
      margin: '0 0 0 56px',
    },
  },
  args: {
    showColorControl: false,
    color: SITE_MENU_COLOR_VARIANTS.PRIMARY,
    ariaLabel: 'Site navigation menu',
  },
  render: renderSiteMenuWithSwitching,
};

export const InteractiveExample: Story = {
  parameters: {
    __sb: {
      height: '100dvh',
      margin: '0 0 0 56px',
    },
  },
  args: {
    showColorControl: true,
    color: SITE_MENU_COLOR_VARIANTS.PRIMARY,

        <col-icon name="dash-board" size="20"></col-icon>

      </col-site-menu-button>
    </col-site-menu-toolbar>
    <layout-demo title="Page Title" showplaceholder></layout-demo>
  `,
};

export const ButtonExampleSelected: Story = {
  tags: ['!dev'],
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        transform: (code: string) => {
          const buttonMatch = code.match(
            /<col-site-menu-button[^>]*>[\s\S]*?<\/col-site-menu-button>/
          );
          return formatCodeString(buttonMatch?.[0] || '');
        },
      },
    },
    __sb: {
      justifyContent: 'center',
      alignItems: 'center',
      height: '150px',
    },
  },
  args: {
    ariaLabel: 'Dashboard',
    selected: true,
  },
  render: (args: SiteMenuStoryArgs) => html`
    <div id="button-selected">
      <div>${renderButton(args)}</div>
    </div>
    <script>
      setTimeout(() => {
        const button = document.querySelector('#button-selected col-site-menu-button');
        if (button) {
          // Mock the context to match the actionId
          button._context = {
            selectedActionId: 'dashboard',
            color: 'primary',
            setFocusedActionElement: () => {},
            selectAction: () => {},
            deselectAllActions: () => {},
          };
          button.requestUpdate();
        }
      }, 0);
    </script>
  `,
};

// Hidden individual stories for MDX Canvas usage
export const SubmenuExample: Story = {
  tags: ['!dev'],
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        transform: (code: string) => {
          // Extract only the submenu component content
          const submenuMatch = code.match(
            /<col-site-menu-submenu[^>]*>[\s\S]*?<\/col-site-menu-submenu>/
          );
          return formatCodeString(submenuMatch?.[0] || '');
        },
      },
    },
    __sb: {
      padding: '1rem',
      justifyContent: 'center',
      alignItems: 'start',
      height: '300px',
    },
  },
  args: {
    ariaLabel: 'Dashboard submenu content',
  },
  render: (args: SiteMenuStoryArgs) => html`
    <div id="submenu-container">
      <col-site-menu-submenu aria-label="${args.ariaLabel}">
        ${unsafeHTML(CategoryTemplates.dashboard())} ${unsafeHTML(SubcategoryTemplates.overview())}
        ${unsafeHTML(SubcategoryTemplates.reports())}
      </col-site-menu-submenu>
    </div>
    <script>
      setTimeout(() => {
        const submenu = document.querySelector('#submenu-container col-site-menu-submenu');
        const subcategory = submenu?.querySelector('col-site-menu-subcategory');
        const asideElement = submenu?.shadowRoot?.querySelector('aside');
        if (submenu) {
          // Set the _previousOpenState to true to prevent focus triggering
          submenu._previousOpenState = true;

          // Mock the context to make submenu visible
          submenu._context = {
            open: true,
          };
          submenu.requestUpdate();
        }
        if (subcategory) {
          // Mock the context to make subcategory expanded
          subcategory._context = {
            openSubcategoryId: 'overview',
          };
          subcategory.requestUpdate();
        }
        if (asideElement) {
          asideElement.style.transition = 'none';
        }
      }, 0);
    </script>
  `,
};

// Hidden individual stories for MDX Canvas usage
export const SubcategoryExampleOpen: Story = {
  tags: ['!dev'],
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        transform: (code: string) => {
          const subcategoryMatch = code.match(
            /<col-site-menu-subcategory[^>]*>[\s\S]*?<\/col-site-menu-subcategory>/
          );
          return formatCodeString(subcategoryMatch?.[0] || '');
        },
      },
    },
    __sb: {
      justifyContent: 'center',
      alignItems: 'center',
      height: '200px',
    },
  },
  args: {
    subcategoryId: 'overview',
    ariaLabel: 'Toggle Overview',
  },
  render: (args: SiteMenuStoryArgs) => html`
    <div id="subcategory-container" style="min-width: 242px">
      ${unsafeHTML(
        SubcategoryTemplates.overview({
          subcategoryId: args.subcategoryId,
          ariaLabel: args.ariaLabel,
        })
      )}
    </div>
    <script>
      setTimeout(() => {
        const subcategory = document.querySelector(
          '#subcategory-container col-site-menu-subcategory'
        );
        if (subcategory) {
          // Mock the context first so the setter can work
          subcategory._context = {
            openSubcategoryId: 'overview',
          };
        }
        // Now the set the open to true to expand
        subcategory.open = true;
        subcategory.requestUpdate();
      }, 0);
    </script>
  `,
};

// Wrapper component to manage state for the interactive examples
@customElement('site-menu-story-wrapper')
class SiteMenuStoryWrapper extends LitElement {
  static styles = css`
    :host {
      display: flex;
      width: 100%;
    }
  `;

  @property()
  color = 'primary';

  @property({ type: Boolean })
  open = false;

  @property()
  ariaLabel = '';

  @state()
  private submenuContent = '';

  @state()
  private selectedActionId = '';

  connectedCallback() {
    super.connectedCallback();
    if (this.open) {
      this.selectedActionId = 'dashboard';
      this.submenuContent = SubmenuContentTemplates.dashboardContent();
    }
  }

  updated(changedProperties: Map<string, unknown>) {
    if (changedProperties.has('open')) {
      if (this.open && !this.submenuContent) {
        this.selectedActionId = 'dashboard';
        this.submenuContent = SubmenuContentTemplates.dashboardContent();
      } else if (!this.open) {
        this.selectedActionId = '';
        this.submenuContent = '';
      }
    }
  }

  private handleActionSelected = (e: CustomEvent) => {
    action('site-menu-action-selected')(e);

    const { actionId } = e.detail;
    this.selectedActionId = actionId;

    if (actionId === 'dashboard') {
      this.submenuContent = SubmenuContentTemplates.dashboardContent();
    } else if (actionId === 'analytics') {
      this.submenuContent = SubmenuContentTemplates.analyticsContent();
    }
  };

  render() {
    return html`
      <col-site-menu
        color="${this.color}"
        ?open=${this.open}
        aria-label="${this.ariaLabel || nothing}"
        @site-menu-action-selected=${this.handleActionSelected}
        @site-menu-submenu-toggle=${action('site-menu-submenu-toggle')}
        @site-menu-subcategory-toggle=${action('site-menu-subcategory-toggle')}
      >
        <col-site-menu-toolbar slot="toolbar">
          <main-logo slot="logo" color="${this.color || 'primary'}" size="36"></main-logo>
          <col-site-menu-button
            slot="actions"
            label="Dashboard"
            action-id="dashboard"
            aria-label="Dashboard"
            ?selected=${this.selectedActionId === 'dashboard'}
          >
            <col-icon name="dash-board" size="20"></col-icon>
          </col-site-menu-button>
          <col-site-menu-button
            slot="actions"
            label="Analytics"
            action-id="analytics"
            aria-label="Analytics"
            ?selected=${this.selectedActionId === 'analytics'}
          >
            <col-icon name="trending-up-square" size="20"></col-icon>
          </col-site-menu-button>
        </col-site-menu-toolbar>
        <col-site-menu-submenu slot="submenu">
          ${unsafeHTML(this.submenuContent)}
        </col-site-menu-submenu>
      </col-site-menu>
      <layout-demo title="Page Title" showplaceholder></layout-demo>
    `;
  }
}

declare global {
  interface HTMLElementTagNameMap {
    'site-menu-story-wrapper': SiteMenuStoryWrapper;
  }
}

FILE: src/components/col-spacer/col-spacer.mdx
================================================
import { Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import { Callout } from '@/_storybook/components';
import * as ColSpacerStories from './col-spacer.stories';

<Meta of={ColSpacerStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Spacer</Title>
    The Colibri Spacer component provides flexible spacing between elements in flex layouts. It automatically expands to fill available space, pushing adjacent elements apart or creating balanced distributions.

    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
      The spacer component uses `flex: 1` to grow and fill available space in flex containers, making it perfect for creating responsive layouts without fixed dimensions.
    </Callout>

    <Subtitle>Component Overview</Subtitle>
    The Spacer component is a simple but powerful layout utility that:

    - **Fills available space** - Automatically expands to use remaining flex space
    - **Pushes elements apart** - Creates separation between flex items
    - **Enables balanced layouts** - Multiple spacers distribute space evenly
    - **Non-interactive** - Has no pointer events or user selection
    - **Accessible** - Properly hidden from screen readers with `aria-hidden="true"`

    <Subtitle>Table of contents</Subtitle>
    - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
    - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
    - [Component preview](#component-preview)
      - [Basic usage](#basic-usage)
      - [Toolbar layouts](#toolbar-layouts)
      - [Multiple spacers](#multiple-spacers)
    - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

    ## How to use it
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, registerAllComponents, ColSpacer } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Option 1: Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColSpacer]);

        // Option 2: Register all Colibri components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <!-- Basic toolbar layout -->
        <div style="display: flex; align-items: center;">
          <button>Menu</button>
          <col-spacer></col-spacer>
          <button>Profile</button>
        </div>

        <!-- Multiple spacers for even distribution -->
        <div style="display: flex; align-items: center;">
          <span>Left</span>
          <col-spacer></col-spacer>
          <span>Center</span>
          <col-spacer></col-spacer>
          <span>Right</span>
        </div>

        <!-- Form actions with spacer -->
        <div style="display: flex; gap: 8px;">
          <button>Help</button>
          <col-spacer></col-spacer>
          <button>Cancel</button>
          <button>Save</button>
        </div>
        ```

    ## When to use it
    The Spacer component is ideal for creating flexible layouts in various interface patterns:

    ### Primary Use Cases

    **Toolbar and Navigation Layouts**
    - Push user actions to the right side of toolbars
    - Separate logo/branding from navigation items
    - Create space between primary and secondary actions

    **Form and Dialog Layouts**
    - Separate help text from action buttons
    - Push confirmation buttons to the right
    - Create balanced button distributions

    **Card and Content Headers**
    - Separate titles from action menus
    - Push timestamps or metadata to the right
    - Balance content across card headers

    **Status and Information Bars**
    - Distribute status indicators evenly
    - Separate different types of information
    - Create responsive information layouts

    ### Layout Patterns

    **Single Spacer Pattern**
    ```html
    [Item A] [Item B] [----SPACER----] [Item C]
    ```
    - Pushes Item C to the far right
    - Items A and B stay grouped on the left

    **Multiple Spacer Pattern**
    ```html
    [Item A] [--SPACER--] [Item B] [--SPACER--] [Item C]
    ```
    - Distributes items evenly across available space
    - Creates balanced, centered layouts

    **Mixed Content Pattern**
    ```html
    [Logo] [Nav Items] [----SPACER----] [User Menu] [Settings]
    ```
    - Separates primary navigation from user actions
    - Maintains grouping while maximizing space usage

    ### Best Practices

    **Container Requirements**
    - Always use within `display: flex` containers
    - Ensure parent has defined width or fills available space
    - Consider `align-items` for vertical alignment

    **Accessibility**
    - Spacer is automatically hidden from screen readers
    - Focus order skips spacer elements naturally
    - No additional ARIA attributes needed

    **Performance**
    - Lightweight component with minimal DOM impact
    - No JavaScript logic or event handlers
    - Pure CSS-based flexible spacing

    **Responsive Design**
    - Works automatically with responsive flex containers
    - Adapts to changing container sizes
    - No media queries or breakpoint logic needed

    ## Component preview

    ### Basic usage
    <div>
      Simple spacer usage showing how it fills available space between flex items.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={ColSpacerStories.BasicUsage} />

    ### Toolbar layouts
    <div>
      Common toolbar patterns using spacers to separate and position elements.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={ColSpacerStories.ToolbarLayout} />

    ### Multiple spacers
    <div>
      Using multiple spacers to create evenly distributed layouts.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={ColSpacerStories.MultipleSpacers} />

    ## Interact with the component
    To see all spacer examples and use cases, navigate to the <LinkTo title={ColSpacerStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.

  </div>
</div>

FILE: src/components/col-spinner/col-spinner.mdx

import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import * as ColSpinnerStories from './col-spinner.stories';

<Meta of={ColSpinnerStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Spinner</Title>
    A spinner is an animated spinning icon that lets users know content is being loaded. Spinners indicate that a system process is going on that will end with content being displayed to the user. They animate as soon as user action is initiated and disappear once the content appears.

    <Subtitle>Table of contents</Subtitle>
    - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
    - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
    - [Component preview](#component-preview)
    - [Accessibility](#accessibility)
    - [Properties](#properties)
    - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

    ## How to use it
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, registerAllComponents, ColSpinner } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Option 1: Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColSpinner]);

        // Option 2: Register all Colibri components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-spinner></col-spinner>
        ```

    ## When to use it
    The `col-spinner` component should be used for short, indeterminate activities (between 2-5 seconds). Each spinner variant has a particular function and its design signals that function to the user. It is therefore very important that the different variants are implemented consistently across products, so that they message the correct actions.

    - **16**: Use within elements such as buttons or form fields, or when there are other space constraints.
    - **24**: The default size which is used for most use cases
    - **48**: The default size that is used for page loading

    **Alignment**

    - **Positioning**: The Spinner can be positioned in different places in the interface, depending on its purpose. It can be centered on the screen, attached to a specific element such as a load button, or even embedded in a content area while processing a task.
    - **Visual feedback**: The Spinner displays an animation or icon that indicates ongoing activity.

    ## Component preview
    You can click on the **Show code** button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component:

    <h4>Default Use Case</h4>
    <Canvas of={ColSpinnerStories.Default}/>

    <h4>Size: Medium</h4>
    <Canvas of={ColSpinnerStories.MediumSpinner} />

    <h4>Size: Large</h4>
    <Canvas of={ColSpinnerStories.LargeSpinner} />

    <h4>Spinner indicator in a ColButton</h4>
    <div>
      In this example, we're using a `col-button` to show how the `col-spinner` can be used to show the user content is loading when the button is clicked. There is a 2s timer simulating loading time to show what the interaction could look like.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={ColSpinnerStories.ButtonWithSpinner} />

    ## Accessibility
    The `col-spinner` component is designed with accessibility in mind, ensuring that all users including those using assistive technologies are politely alerted that content is loading.

    - **ARIA Attributes:**
      - The `accessibility-text` property allows developers to update the text that is read to users when a screen reader is being used.
        - The default value is `Loading`
        - Under the hood this maps to two things:
            - `aria-live` which is set to `polite` which tells screen readers to announce updates to the content **only** when the user is idle, so it doesn't interrupt their current task.
            - `span.visually-hidden` tag which screen-readers look for to get the text they read, this is where the `accessibility-text` content goes.
    - **Screen Reader Support**
      - The `accessibility-text` property is what tells the reader what to say; the default value is `Loading`.
    - **Best Practices**
      - In the event of using the spinner while submitting a form, saving information, etc; it would be ideal to use `accessibility-text` to convey that message accurately by setting it to `Submitting` or `Saving`.

    ## Properties

    <ArgTypes of={ColSpinnerStories} />

    ## Interact with the component
    To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={ColSpinnerStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.

  </div>
</div>

FILE: src/components/col-spinner/col-spinner.stories.tsx

import { html, render as LitRenderer, nothing } from 'lit';
import type { ColibriStoryMeta, ColibriStory } from '@/types/storybook';
import { SPINNER_SIZES } from '@telesign/colibri';
import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils/formatters';

type StoryArgs = {
  accessibilityText: string;
  size: SPINNER_SIZES;
};

const meta = {
  title: 'Feedback/Spinner',
  component: 'col-spinner',
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        excludeDecorators: true,
        transform: formatCodeString,
      },
    },
  },
  argTypes: {
    accessibilityText: {
      name: 'accessibility-text',
      control: 'text',
      description: 'The text that is read on screen-readers to indicate loading/busy state',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'Loading' },
      },
    },
    size: {
      control: 'select',
      options: Object.values(SPINNER_SIZES),
      description: 'The size the spinner should be: 16, 24 or 48',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: '16' },
      },
    },
  },
  args: {
    size: SPINNER_SIZES.SMALL,
    accessibilityText: 'Loading',
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<StoryArgs>;

export default meta;

type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

const renderSpinner: Story['render'] = ({ accessibilityText, size }) => html`
  <col-spinner accessibility-text=${accessibilityText} size=${size}></col-spinner>
`;

/**
 * Default story showing a small Spinner
 */
export const Default: Story = {
  render: ({ accessibilityText, size }) => html`
    <col-spinner size=${size} accessibility-text=${accessibilityText}></col-spinner>
  `,
};

/**
 * This story showcases a medium size ColSpinner
 */
export const MediumSpinner: Story = {
  render: renderSpinner,
  args: {
    size: SPINNER_SIZES.MEDIUM,
  },
};

/**
 * This story showcases a large size ColSpinner
 */
export const LargeSpinner: Story = {
  render: renderSpinner,
  args: {
    size: SPINNER_SIZES.LARGE,
  },
};

/**
 * This story showcases a ColSpinner in a ColButton
 */
export const ButtonWithSpinner: Story = {
  render: () => {
    let isLoading = false;
    const container = document.createElement('div');
    const load = () => {
      isLoading = true;
      update();

      setTimeout(() => {
        isLoading = false;
        update();
      }, 2000);
    };

    const update = () => {
      LitRenderer(
        html`
          <col-button
            name="load-button"
            color="primary"
            variant="outlined"
            ?disabled=${isLoading}
            @click=${load}
          >
            ${isLoading ? 'Loading...' : 'Get more results'}
            ${isLoading ? html`<col-spinner></col-spinner>` : nothing}
          </col-button>
        `,
        container
      );
    };

    update();
    return container;
  },
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        code: `// in the JS file we add out state and event handler
    let isLoading = false;
    const load = () => {
      isLoading = true;
      update();

      setTimeout(() => {
        isLoading = false;
        update();
      }, 2000);
    };

    // html
    <col-button
        name="load-button"
        color="primary"
        variant="outlined"
        ?disabled=\${isLoading}
        @click=\${load}
    >
        \${isLoading ? 'Loading...' : 'Get more results'}
        \${isLoading ? html\`<col-spinner></col-spinner>\` : nothing}
    </col-button>`,
      },
    },
  },
};
