import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import * as ColStatusStories from './col-status.stories';

<Meta of={ColStatusStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Status</Title>
    An indicator highlights a page element and informs the user of something that needs the user’s attention. Often, the indicator will denote that there has been a change to a particular item. They are frequently used to signal validation errors or notifications, changes, or updates. They can also be used on their own. Indicators are visual cues intended to attract the user’s attention to a piece of content or UI element that is dynamic in nature.

    <Subtitle>How to use it</Subtitle>
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, registerAllComponents, ColStatus } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Option 1: Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColStatus]);

        // Option 2: Register all Colibri components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-status status="inprogress">
          <col-icon name="check-circle-fill"></col-icon>
          In Progress
        </col-status>
        ```

    <Subtitle>Use cases</Subtitle>
    The Status component is a key tool for quickly communicating the status of a system, task, or process. Several common use cases for this component are described below:

    - **System Status Indicator:** The Status component is used to display the current status of a system or service.
    - **Status Notifications:** The Status component can be used to provide feedback on data validation (correct, incorrect, under review).
    - **Workflow Progress Indicator:** In complex workflows, the Status component can indicate the current status of each step (started, in progress, completed).
    - **Status Monitoring in Administration Panels:** In administration panels, the Status component can show the status of different services or system components (active, inactive, with problems).

    <Subtitle>Component preview</Subtitle>
    You can click on the **Show code** button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component:

FILE: src/components/col-status/col-status.stories.tsx
================================================
import { html } from 'lit';
import type { ColibriStoryMeta, ColibriStory } from '@/types/storybook';
import { STATUS_STATES } from '@telesign/colibri';
import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils/formatters';

type StoryArgs = {
  dot: boolean;
  status: string;
  statusText: string;
  iconName: string;
};

const meta = {
  title: 'Feedback/Status',
  component: 'col-status',
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        excludeDecorators: true,
        transform: formatCodeString,
      },
    },
  },
  argTypes: {
    status: {
      control: 'select',
      options: Object.values(STATUS_STATES),
      description: 'The status state of the component',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'neutral' },
      },
    },
    dot: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Whether to display a visual dot indicator',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'dot', neq: false },
    },
    statusText: {
      control: 'text',
      description:
        'The text content to display in the status. **Storybook control only, not a component prop.**',
      table: {
        category: 'Storybook',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'Neutral' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'statusText', neq: '' },
    },
    iconName: {
      control: 'text',
      description:
        'Name of the icon to display in the status. **Storybook control only, not a component prop.**',
      table: {
        category: 'Storybook',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: '' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'iconName', neq: '' },
    },
  },
  args: {
    status: STATUS_STATES.NEUTRAL,
    dot: false,
    statusText: '',
    iconName: '',
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<StoryArgs>;

export default meta;

type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

const renderStatus: Story['render'] = ({ dot, status, statusText, iconName }) => html`
  <col-status status=${status} ?dot=${dot}>
    ${iconName ? html`<col-icon name=${iconName}></col-icon>` : ''} ${statusText}
  </col-status>
`;

/**
 * Default story showing a Status component without dot or icon
 */
export const Default: Story = {
  render: ({ dot, status }) => html`
    <col-status ?dot=${dot} status=${status}>Neutral</col-status>
  `,
};

/**
 * This story showcases an Neutral Status component with icon
 */
export const StatusNeutral: Story = {
  args: {
    status: STATUS_STATES.NEUTRAL,
    statusText: 'Neutral',
    iconName: 'check-circle-fill',
  },
  render: renderStatus,
};

/**
 * This story showcases an Pending Status component with icon
 */
export const StatusPending: Story = {
  args: {
    status: STATUS_STATES.PENDING,
    statusText: 'To be approved',
    iconName: 'three-dots-circle-fill',
  },
  render: renderStatus,
};

/**
 * This story showcases an In Progress Status component with icon
 */
export const StatusInProgress: Story = {
  args: {
    status: STATUS_STATES.INPROGRESS,
    statusText: 'In Progress',
    iconName: 'a-third-circle',
  },
  render: renderStatus,
};

export const StatusCancel: Story = {
  args: {
    status: STATUS_STATES.CANCEL,
    statusText: 'Cancelled',
    iconName: 'close-circle',
  },
  render: renderStatus,
};

/**
 * This story showcases an Complete Status component with icon
 */
export const StatusComplete: Story = {
  args: {
    status: STATUS_STATES.COMPLETE,
    statusText: 'Finalized',
    iconName: 'check-circle',
  },
  render: renderStatus,
};

/**
 * This story showcases a Status component with a dot
 */
export const StatusWithDot: Story = {
  args: {
    dot: true,
    statusText: 'Neutral',
  },
  render: renderStatus,
};

/**
 * This story showcases a Status Pending component with a dot
 */
export const StatusPendingWithDot: Story = {
  args: {
    status: STATUS_STATES.PENDING,
    statusText: 'To be approved',
    dot: true,
  },
  render: renderStatus,

FILE: src/components/col-stepper/col-stepper.mdx
================================================
import { Meta, Title, Subtitle, Canvas, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import * as ColStepperStories from './col-stepper.stories';

<Meta of={ColStepperStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">

  <Title>Stepper</Title>
  The `col-stepper` component helps users navigate **multi-step processes** by visually indicating **progress, completion,** and **errors**.  
  This documentation covers its orientations, step states, and behavior.

  <Subtitle>Table of contents</Subtitle>
  - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
  - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
  - [Component preview](#component-preview)
  - [Layouts](#layouts)
  - [Step states and behaviors](#step-states-and-behaviors)
  - [Accessibility](#accessibility)
  - [Properties](#properties)
  - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

  ## How to use it

  1. Install the package:
      ```bash
      npm install @telesign/colibri
      ```

  2. Import the dependencies:
      ```ts
      import { registerColibriComponents, registerAllComponents, ColStepper, ColStep } from '@telesign/colibri';
      import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
      ```

  3. Register the components:
      ```typescript
      // Option 1: Register specific components
      registerColibriComponents([ColStepper, ColStep]);

      // Option 2: Register all Colibri components
      registerAllComponents();
      ```

  4. Use it in your HTML:
      ```html
      <col-stepper orientation="horizontal" aria-label="Step Progress">
        <col-step step="1" label="Step 1" state="completed" orientation="horizontal" hasconnector></col-step>
        <col-step step="2" label="Step 2" state="active" orientation="horizontal" hasconnector></col-step>
        <col-step step="3" label="Step 3" state="pending" orientation="horizontal" hasconnector></col-step>
        <col-step step="4" label="Step 4" state="error"></col-step>
      </col-stepper>
      ```

  ## When to use it

  Use the `col-stepper` component when you need to guide users through **multi-step workflows** such as:

  - Multi-page forms or onboarding sequences  
  - Order, setup, or registration processes  
  - Any scenario that requires visual indication of **progress and completion**  

  ## Component preview

  <Canvas of={ColStepperStories.Default} />

  ## Layouts

  ### Horizontal layout

  <div>
    The default orientation arranges steps from left to right.  
    Commonly used for wizards or progress trackers at the top of a page.
  </div>
  <Canvas of={ColStepperStories.Horizontal} />

  ### Vertical layout

  <div>
    Steps are arranged from top to bottom.  
    Ideal for forms or side navigation where vertical space is preferred.
  </div>
  <Canvas of={ColStepperStories.Vertical} />

  ### Custom accessible label

  <div>
    You can provide a custom <code>aria-label</code> to describe the stepper group for assistive technologies.
  </div>
  <Canvas of={ColStepperStories.WithCustomLabel} />

  ## Step states and behaviors

  The Stepper can represent multiple **states** for each step:

  - **Pending:** Step not yet reached or completed.
  - **Active:** Current step the user is on.
  - **Completed:** Step successfully finished.
  - **Error:** Step encountered an issue.
  - **Completed with Error:** Step finished but with an error.

  <Canvas of={ColStepperStories.Default} />

  ## Accessibility

  <div>
    The `col-stepper` is built with accessibility in mind, ensuring assistive technologies can correctly interpret and navigate the component.
  </div>

  - **ARIA Roles:** Uses <code>role="group"</code> to represent a collection of steps.  
  - **ARIA Orientation:** Automatically sets <code>aria-orientation</code> to match layout direction.  
  - **Labeling:** Accepts a descriptive <code>aria-label</code> to announce the step group’s purpose. 

  ## Properties

  <ArgTypes of={ColStepperStories} />

  ## Interact with the component

  To experiment with orientations and labels, visit the <LinkTo title={ColStepperStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section in the Canvas tab.
  </div>
</div>

FILE: src/components/col-stepper/col-stepper.stories.tsx
================================================
import { html } from 'lit';
import type { ColibriStoryMeta, ColibriStory } from '@/types/storybook';
import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils';

type StoryArgs = {
  orientation: 'horizontal' | 'vertical';
  ariaLabel: string;
};

const meta = {
  title: 'Navigation/Stepper',
  component: 'col-stepper',
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        excludeDecorators: true,
        transform: formatCodeString,
      },
    },
    __sb: {
      display: 'flex',
      justifyContent: 'center',
    },
  },
  argTypes: {
    orientation: {
      control: 'select',
      options: ['horizontal', 'vertical'],
      description: 'Defines the layout direction of the steps.',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: "'horizontal' | 'vertical'" },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'horizontal' },
      },
    },
    ariaLabel: {
      name: 'aria-label',
      control: 'text',
      description: 'Provides an accessible label for the stepper group.',
      table: {
        category: 'Accessibility',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'stepper' },
      },
    },
  },
  args: {
    orientation: 'horizontal',
    ariaLabel: 'Step Progress',
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<StoryArgs>;

export default meta;
type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

const renderStepper: Story['render'] = args => html`
  <col-stepper orientation=${args.orientation} aria-label=${args.ariaLabel}>
    <col-step step="1" label="Step 1" state="pending" orientation=${args.orientation} ?hasconnector=${true}></col-step>
    <col-step step="2" label="Step 2" state="active" orientation=${args.orientation} ?hasconnector=${true}></col-step>
    <col-step step="3" label="Step 3" state="completed" orientation=${args.orientation} ?hasconnector=${true}></col-step>
    <col-step step="4" label="Step 4" state="error" orientation=${args.orientation} ?hasconnector=${true}></col-step>
    <col-step step="5" label="Step 5" state="completed_error" orientation=${args.orientation}></col-step>
  </col-stepper>
`;

export const Default: Story = {
  args: {
    orientation: 'horizontal',
  },
  render: renderStepper,
};

export const Horizontal: Story = {
  name: 'Horizontal layout',
  args: {
    orientation: 'horizontal',
  },
  render: renderStepper,
};

export const Vertical: Story = {
  name: 'Vertical layout',
  args: {
    orientation: 'vertical',
  },
  render: renderStepper,
};

export const WithCustomLabel: Story = {
  name: 'Custom accessible label',
  args: {
    ariaLabel: 'User registration steps',
  },
  render: renderStepper,
};

FILE: src/components/col-table/col-table.mdx
================================================
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import { Callout } from '@/_storybook/components';
import * as ColTableStories from './col-table.stories';

<Meta of={ColTableStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Table</Title>
    The `col-table` component provides a structured layout for displaying tabular data with support for selection, scrolling, and custom content.

    <Subtitle>Table of contents</Subtitle>
    - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
      - [Installation](#installation)
      - [Table Structure](#table-structure)
    - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
      - [Best Practices](#best-practices)
    - [Component preview](#component-preview)
      - [Basic Table](#basic-table)
      - [Selectable Rows](#selectable-rows)
      - [Scrollable Table](#scrollable-table)
      - [Empty State](#empty-state)
      - [Complex Cell Content](#complex-cell-content)
      - [Custom Column Widths](#custom-column-widths)
      - [Full Featured Example](#full-featured-example)
    - [Table Components](#table-components)
      - [col-table-head](#col-table-head)
      - [col-table-body](#col-table-body)
      - [col-table-row](#col-table-row)
      - [col-table-cell](#col-table-cell)
    - [Properties](#properties)
    - [Events](#events)
    - [CSS Custom Properties](#css-custom-properties)
    - [Accessibility](#accessibility)
    - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

    ## How to use it

    ### Installation
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import {
          registerColibriComponents,
          ColTable,
          ColTableHead,
          ColTableBody,
          ColTableRow,
          ColTableCell
        } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColTable, ColTableHead, ColTableBody, ColTableRow, ColTableCell]);

        // Or register all components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-table>
          <col-table-head>
            <col-table-cell header>Name</col-table-cell>
            <col-table-cell header>Email</col-table-cell>
            <col-table-cell header>Status</col-table-cell>
          </col-table-head>
          <col-table-body>
            <col-table-row>
              <col-table-cell>John Doe</col-table-cell>
              <col-table-cell>john@example.com</col-table-cell>
              <col-table-cell>Active</col-table-cell>
            </col-table-row>
          </col-table-body>
        </col-table>
        ```

    ### Table Structure

    The table component system consists of five main components that work together:

    | Component | Purpose | Parent | Children |
    |-----------|---------|--------|----------|
    | `col-table` | Main container | - | `col-table-head`, `col-table-body` |
    | `col-table-head` | Header section | `col-table` | `col-table-cell` |
    | `col-table-body` | Data section | `col-table` | `col-table-row` |
    | `col-table-row` | Table row | `col-table-head`, `col-table-body` | `col-table-cell` |
    | `col-table-cell` | Individual cell | `col-table-row` | Any content |

    ## When to use it

    The table component is ideal for displaying structured data that needs to be:

    * **Compared** - Side-by-side comparison of multiple data points
    * **Sorted** - Organizing data by different criteria (visual indicators shown, sorting logic not built-in)
    * **Filtered** - Finding specific records in large datasets
    * **Selected** - Performing bulk actions on multiple rows
    * **Scanned** - Quick visual scanning of tabular information

    Common use cases include:

    * **User Management** - Lists of users with roles and permissions
    * **Order Processing** - E-commerce orders with status tracking
    * **Data Reports** - Financial or analytical data presentation
    * **Inventory Systems** - Product listings with stock information
    * **Task Lists** - Project management with assignees and deadlines
    * **Log Viewers** - System logs and audit trails

    ### Best Practices

    * **Keep headers concise** - Use short, descriptive column headers
    * **Align data appropriately** - Left-align text, right-align numbers
    * **Provide visual feedback** - Use hover states and selection indicators
    * **Consider mobile** - Use horizontal scrolling for wide tables on small screens
    * **Limit columns** - Show only essential columns, provide details in expandable rows or modals
    * **Use semantic markup** - Proper table structure helps with accessibility
    * **Add loading states** - Show skeletons or spinners while data loads
    * **Handle empty states** - Provide helpful messages when no data is available

    ## Component preview
    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    ### Basic Table
    A simple table with headers and data rows.

    <Canvas of={ColTableStories.Default} />

    ### Selectable Rows
    Enable row selection with checkboxes for bulk actions.

    <Canvas of={ColTableStories.SelectableRows} />

    - When `selectable` is true, checkboxes are automatically added to each row
    - The header checkbox selects/deselects all rows
    - Individual rows can be selected by clicking anywhere on the row

    ### Scrollable Table
    Set a maximum height to enable vertical scrolling for long tables.

    <Canvas of={ColTableStories.ScrollableTable} />

    - Set `max-height` to define the scrollable area height
    - Set `scroller` to true to enable the scroll behavior
    - The header remains sticky while scrolling

    ### Empty State
    The table automatically displays a "No data" message when the body is empty.

    <Canvas of={ColTableStories.EmptyTable} />

    ### Complex Cell Content
    Table cells can contain any content including multiple components.

    <Canvas of={ColTableStories.ComplexCellContent} />

    ### Custom Column Widths
    Control column widths using the `--cell-flex` CSS variable.

    <Canvas of={ColTableStories.CustomColumnWidths} />

    ```css
    /* Fixed width column */
    col-table-cell {
      --cell-flex: 0 0 150px;
    }

    /* Flexible column with proportional sizing */
    col-table-cell {
      --cell-flex: 2 1 0%; /* Takes 2x space compared to flex: 1 */
    }
    ```

    ### Full Featured Example
    Complete example combining selection, scrolling, headers, footers, and pagination.

    <Canvas of={ColTableStories.FullFeaturedExample} />

    ## Table Components

    ### col-table-head

    The table header component contains the column headers.

    **Properties:**
    - None (container component)

    **Slots:**
    - Default slot: Accepts `col-table-row` containing header cells

    **Usage:**
    ```html
    <col-table-head>
       <col-table-cell header>Column 1</col-table-cell>
       <col-table-cell header>Column 2</col-table-cell>
    </col-table-head>
    ```

    ### col-table-body

    The table body component contains the data rows.

    **Properties:**
    - None (container component)

    **Slots:**
    - Default slot: Accepts `col-table-row` elements

    **Features:**
    - Automatically shows "No data to be displayed" when empty
    - Scrollable when parent table has `max-height` set

    **Usage:**
    ```html
    <col-table-body>
      <col-table-row>
        <col-table-cell>Data 1</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>Data 2</col-table-cell>
      </col-table-row>
    </col-table-body>
    ```

    ### col-table-row

    Individual table row component that can be selectable.

    **Properties:**
    | Property | Type | Default | Description |
    |----------|------|---------|-------------|
    | `row-index` | `number` | - | The index of this row in the table |
    | `selectable` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether the row can be selected |
    | `selected` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether the row is currently selected |

    **Events:**
    - `row-selected`: Fired when a row is clicked/selected

    **Usage:**
    ```html
    <col-table-row row-index="1" selectable>
      <col-table-cell>Cell content</col-table-cell>
    </col-table-row>
    ```

    ### col-table-cell

    Individual table cell component for data or headers.

    **Properties:**
    | Property | Type | Default | Description |
    |----------|------|---------|-------------|
    | `header` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether the cell is a header cell |

    **CSS Custom Properties:**
    - `--cell-flex`: Controls the flex properties of the cell (default: `1 1 0%`)

    **Usage:**
    ```html
    <!-- Header cell -->
    <col-table-cell header>Column Name</col-table-cell>

    <!-- Data cell -->
    <col-table-cell>Cell Data</col-table-cell>

    <!-- Cell with custom width -->
    <col-table-cell style="--cell-flex: 0 0 200px;">Fixed Width</col-table-cell>
    ```

    ## Properties
    <ArgTypes of={ColTableStories} />

    ## Events

    ### col-table Events

    | Event | Description | Detail |
    |-------|-------------|--------|
    | `all-rows-selected` | Fired when the header checkbox is toggled | `{ rows: ColTableRow[], element: ColTable, selectable: boolean }` |

    ### col-table-row Events

    | Event | Description | Detail |
    |-------|-------------|--------|
    | `row-selected` | Fired when a row is clicked/selected | `{ rowIndex: number, selectable: boolean, selected: boolean, element: ColTableRow }` |

    ## CSS Custom Properties

    The table components expose several CSS custom properties for customization:

    ### col-table
    - `--col-table-min-height`: Minimum height for the table content area
    - `--col-table-max-height`: Maximum height for the table content area (enables scrolling)

    ### col-table-cell
    - `--cell-flex`: Controls the flex properties of the cell (e.g., `1 1 0%`, `0 0 200px`)

    Example:
    ```css
    col-table {
      --col-table-max-height: 500px;
      --col-table-min-height: 200px;
    }

    col-table-cell {
      --cell-flex: 2 1 0%; /* Cell takes 2x space */
    }
    ```

    ## Accessibility

    The Table component is built with accessibility in mind:

    * **Semantic Structure**: Uses proper ARIA roles (`table`, `rowgroup`, `row`, `cell`)
    * **Keyboard Navigation**:
      - Rows are focusable with `tabindex="0"`
      - Selection can be triggered via keyboard
    * **ARIA Attributes**:
      - `aria-label` for describing table sections
      - `aria-selected` for indicating selected rows
      - `aria-rowindex` for row position
      - `aria-rowcount` and `aria-colcount` for table dimensions
    * **Screen Reader Support**: Proper heading structure and descriptive labels
    * **Focus Management**: Clear focus indicators on interactive elements

    ### Keyboard Support

    | Key | Action |
    |-----|--------|
    | `Tab` | Move focus between interactive elements |
    | `Space` | Select/deselect focused row (when selectable) |
    | `Enter` | Activate buttons or links within cells |

    <Subtitle>Interact with the component</Subtitle>
    To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={ColTableStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.

  </div>
</div>



================================================
FILE: src/components/col-table/col-table.stories.tsx
================================================
import { html, nothing } from 'lit';
import type { ColibriStoryMeta, ColibriStory } from '@/types/storybook';
import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils/formatters';
import { fn } from '@storybook/test';

type StoryArgs = {
  maxHeight: string;
  minHeight: string;
  checkboxCellWidth: string;
  selectable: boolean;
  scroller: boolean;
  onAllRowsSelected: () => void;
  onRowSelected: () => void;
};

const meta = {
  title: 'Data Visualization/Table',
  component: 'col-table',
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        excludeDecorators: true,
        transform: formatCodeString,
      },
    },
  },
  argTypes: {
    maxHeight: {
      name: 'max-height',
      control: 'text',
      description:
        'Maximum height CSS value for the table content area. When exceeded, enables vertical scrolling.',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'undefined' },
      },
    },
    minHeight: {
      name: 'min-height',
      control: 'text',
      description: 'Minimum height CSS value for the table content area.',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'auto' },
      },
    },
    checkboxCellWidth: {
      name: 'checkbox-cell-width',
      control: 'text',
      description:
        'Defines the column width for the checkbox in each row when selectable is enabled.',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: '48px' },
      },
    },
    selectable: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description:
        'Whether the table rows are allowed to be selected. Automatically adds checkboxes to rows.',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
    },
    scroller: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Whether the table has vertical scroll (requires max-height value to be set).',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
    },
    onAllRowsSelected: {
      action: 'all-rows-selected',
      description:
        'Fired when the header checkbox is checked/unchecked, selecting or deselecting all rows.',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
      },
    },
    onRowSelected: {
      action: 'row-selected',
      description: 'Fired when a table row is clicked/selected.',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
      },
    },
  },
  args: {
    maxHeight: '',
    minHeight: 'auto',
    checkboxCellWidth: '',
    selectable: false,
    scroller: false,
    onAllRowsSelected: fn(),
    onRowSelected: fn(),
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<StoryArgs>;

export default meta;

type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

const renderTable: Story['render'] = ({
  maxHeight,
  minHeight,
  checkboxCellWidth,
  selectable,
  scroller,
  onAllRowsSelected,
  onRowSelected,
}) => html`
  <col-table
    max-height=${maxHeight || nothing}
    min-height=${minHeight || nothing}
    checkbox-cell-width=${checkboxCellWidth || nothing}
    ?selectable=${selectable}
    ?scroller=${scroller}
    @all-rows-selected=${onAllRowsSelected}
    @row-selected=${onRowSelected}
  >
    <col-table-head>
      <col-table-cell header>Name</col-table-cell>
      <col-table-cell header>Email</col-table-cell>
      <col-table-cell header>Status</col-table-cell>
      <col-table-cell header>Role</col-table-cell>
      <col-table-cell header>Actions</col-table-cell>
    </col-table-head>
    <col-table-body>
      <col-table-row row-index="1">
        <col-table-cell>John Doe</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>john.doe@example.com</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>
          <col-badge variant="success">Active</col-badge>
        </col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>Administrator</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>
          <col-button size="small">Edit</col-button>
        </col-table-cell>
      </col-table-row>
      <col-table-row row-index="2">
        <col-table-cell>Jane Smith</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>jane.smith@example.com</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>
          <col-badge variant="warning">Pending</col-badge>
        </col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>Editor</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>
          <col-button size="small">Edit</col-button>
        </col-table-cell>
      </col-table-row>
      <col-table-row row-index="3">
        <col-table-cell>Mike Johnson</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>mike.johnson@example.com</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>
          <col-badge variant="success">Active</col-badge>
        </col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>Viewer</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell>
          <col-button size="small">Edit</col-button>
        </col-table-cell>
      </col-table-row>
    </col-table-body>
  </col-table>
`;

/**
 * Default story showing a basic table with headers and data rows
 */
export const Default: Story = {
  render: renderTable,
};

/**
 * Table with selectable rows showing checkboxes for row selection
 */
export const SelectableRows: Story = {
  args: {
    selectable: true,
  },
  render: renderTable,
};

/**
 * Table with scrollable content when max-height is exceeded
 */
export const ScrollableTable: Story = {
  args: {
    maxHeight: '300px',
    scroller: true,
  },
  render: args => html`
    <col-table
      max-height=${args.maxHeight || nothing}
      min-height=${args.minHeight || nothing}
      checkbox-cell-width=${args.checkboxCellWidth || nothing}
      ?selectable=${args.selectable}
      ?scroller=${args.scroller}
      @all-rows-selected=${args.onAllRowsSelected}
      @row-selected=${args.onRowSelected}
    >
      <col-table-head>
        <col-table-cell header>Name</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell header>Email</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell header>Department</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell header>Status</col-table-cell>
        <col-table-cell header>Actions</col-table-cell>
      </col-table-head>
      <col-table-body>
        ${[...Array(10)].map(
          (_, i) => html`
            <col-table-row row-index="${i + 1}">
              <col-table-cell>Employee ${i + 1}</col-table-cell>
              <col-table-cell>employee${i + 1}@example.com</col-table-cell>
              <col-table-cell>Department ${(i % 3) + 1}</col-table-cell>
              <col-table-cell>
                <col-badge variant="${i % 2 === 0 ? 'success' : 'warning'}">
                  ${i % 2 === 0 ? 'Active' : 'Pending'}
                </col-badge>
              </col-table-cell>
              <col-table-cell>
                <col-button size="small">Edit</col-button>
              </col-table-cell>
            </col-table-row>
          `
        )}
      </col-table-body>
    </col-table>
  `,
};

/**
 * Full-featured table example with all features combined
 */
export const FullFeaturedExample: Story = {
  args: {
    selectable: true,
    scroller: true,
    maxHeight: '500px',
  },
  parameters: {
    controls: { disable: true },
  },
  render: () => {
    const handleRowSelected = (e: CustomEvent) => {
      console.log('Row selected:', e.detail);
    };

    const handleAllRowsSelected = (e: CustomEvent) => {
      console.log('All rows selected:', e.detail);
    };

    return html`
      <style>
        col-toolbar::part(toolbar) {
          padding: 0;
        }
      </style>
      <col-table
        max-height="500px"
        min-height="300px"
        selectable
        scroller
        @row-selected=${handleRowSelected}
        @all-rows-selected=${handleAllRowsSelected}
      >
        <div slot="table-header">
          <col-toolbar gap="none">
            <div
              style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; gap: 2px"
            >
              <col-icon name="file" size="20"></col-icon>
              <col-typography variant="subheading">
                Order Management
                <col-tooltip slot="icon" position="top">
                  <col-icon name="info-circle" size="14px"></col-icon>
                  <span slot="tooltip-content">Track and manage customer orders</span>
                </col-tooltip>
              </col-typography>
            </div>
            <col-spacer></col-spacer>
            <div
              style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; gap: 2px"
            >
              <col-group orientation="horizontal" withoutgap>
                <col-search-bar
                  placeholder="Type to search..."
                  mode="static"
                  custom-width="100%"
                ></col-search-bar>
                <col-button size="small">
                  <col-icon name="filter" size="16px"></col-icon>
                </col-button>
                <col-button size="small">
                  <col-icon name="table" size="16px"></col-icon>
                </col-button>
              </col-group>
            </div>
          </col-toolbar>
        </div>

        <col-table-head>
          <col-table-cell header>Order #</col-table-cell>
          <col-table-cell header>Customer</col-table-cell>
          <col-table-cell header>Date</col-table-cell>
          <col-table-cell header>Items</col-table-cell>
          <col-table-cell header>Total</col-table-cell>
          <col-table-cell header>Status</col-table-cell>
          <col-table-cell header>Actions</col-table-cell>
        </col-table-head>
        <col-table-body>
          ${[...Array(10)].map(
            (_, i) => html`
              <col-table-row row-index="${i + 1}" ?selectable=${true}>
                <col-table-cell>ORD-${String(1001 + i).padStart(4, '0')}</col-table-cell>
                <col-table-cell>
                  <div>
                    <div>Customer ${i + 1}</div>
                    <div style="font-size: 12px; color: #666;">customer${i + 1}@example.com</div>
                  </div>
                </col-table-cell>
                <col-table-cell>Jan ${15 - i}, 2024</col-table-cell>
                <col-table-cell>${Math.floor(Math.random() * 10) + 1} items</col-table-cell>
                <col-table-cell> $${(Math.random() * 1000 + 100).toFixed(2)} </col-table-cell>
                <col-table-cell>
                  <col-badge
                    variant="${i % 3 === 0 ? 'success' : i % 3 === 1 ? 'warning' : 'default'}"
                  >
                    ${i % 3 === 0 ? 'Shipped' : i % 3 === 1 ? 'Processing' : 'Pending'}
                  </col-badge>
                </col-table-cell>
                <col-table-cell>
                  <col-dropdown>
                    <col-button slot="trigger" size="small" variant="outlined">
                      Actions
                      <col-icon name="chevron-down" size="12"></col-icon>
                    </col-button>
                    <col-list-menu>
                      <col-list-menu-item>View Details</col-list-menu-item>
                      <col-list-menu-item>Edit Order</col-list-menu-item>
                      <col-list-menu-item>Send Invoice</col-list-menu-item>
                      <col-list-menu-item>Cancel Order</col-list-menu-item>
                    </col-list-menu>
                  </col-dropdown>
                </col-table-cell>
              </col-table-row>
            `
          )}
        </col-table-body>

        <div slot="table-footer">
          <col-toolbar gap="none">
            <col-toolbar gap="small">
              <span class="text-xs">Items per page</span>
              <col-select id="items-per-page" value="10" custom-width="60px">
                <col-list-menu>
                  <col-list-menu-item value="5">5</col-list-menu-item>
                  <col-list-menu-item value="10">10</col-list-menu-item>
                  <col-list-menu-item value="20">20</col-list-menu-item>
                  <col-list-menu-item value="50">50</col-list-menu-item>
                  <col-list-menu-item value="100">100</col-list-menu-item>
                </col-list-menu>
              </col-select>
              <col-divider orientation="vertical" style="height: 32px"></col-divider>
              <span id="items-info" class="text-xs" role="status" aria-live="polite"></span>
            </col-toolbar>
            <col-paginator id="paginator" length="4" value="1" align="end"></col-paginator>
          </col-toolbar>
        </div>
      </col-table>
    `;
  },
};

FILE: src/components/col-tag/col-tag.mdx
================================================
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import { Callout } from '@/_storybook/components';
import * as stories from './col-tag.stories';

<Meta of={stories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Tag</Title>
    The `col-tag` component is a small visual entity used to represent and classify information in a tagged manner.

    <Subtitle>Table of contents</Subtitle>
    - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
    - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
    - [Component preview](#component-preview)
      - [Basic Tag](#basic-tag)
      - [With Custom Text label](#with-custom-text-label)
      - [Tag Group by `col-group`](#tag-group-by-col-group)
      - [Variants](#variants)
    - [States](#states)
      - [Disabled](#disabled)
      - [Multi Select](#multi-select)
      - [Read Only](#read-only)
    - [Events](#events)
      - [Removal](#removal)
    - [Accessibility](#accessibility)
    - [Properties](#properties)
    - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

    ## How to use it
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @telesign/colibri
        npm install @telesign/colibri-icons
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, registerAllComponents, ColTag } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import { ColIcon } from '@telesign/colibri-icons';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Option 1: Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColTag, ColIcon]);

        // Option 2: Register all Colibri components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-tag variant="orange" text="Custom Option">
          <col-icon slot="icon" name="emoji-circle" size="16px"></col-icon>
        </col-tag>
        ```

    ## When to use it
    The `col-tag` component is a design element used to display short, descriptive information, usually associated with an object or category. Some use cases are described below:

    **Content Categorization**:
    - **Filter tags**: On search pages to help users filter results.

    **Status Indicators**:
    - **Task Status**: In project management applications to indicate the status of a task (e.g. 'Pending', 'In Progress', 'Completed').
    - **Message notifications**: To indicate the status of a message or notification (e.g. 'Read', 'Unread').
    - **User Profile**: To display a user's roles on the platform.


    Tag groups are sets of `col-tag` elements grouped together to provide greater organisation and context. They are used in the following scenarios:

    <Callout  variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
      There is no such element as `col-tag-group` or similar. To represent tag groups it's meant to use the `col-group` as the parent component. Check out the [Tag Group](#tag-group-by-col-group) example.
    </Callout>

    **Complex category management**:
    - **Admin Panels**: To manage complex categories in a CMS (e.g. product categories and subcategories).
    - **Project management applications**: To group tasks by different statuses or products.

    **Navigation and advanced filtering**:
    - **Search systems**: Group filters by characteristics.
    - **Data analysis applications**: To view multiple dimensions or categories of data (e.g. grouping by years, regions, products).


    ## Component preview

    The following examples showcase the core features and visual options of the `col-tag` component.
    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    ### Basic Tag
    The most fundamental tag does not required any label or attribute to display.
    <Canvas of={stories.Default} />

    ### With Custom Text label
    Use the `text` property to display a custom text label inside the tag component. If not specified the default will be 'Option'.
    <Canvas of={stories.WithCustomText} />

    ### With Icon
    The component supports an icon slot and in order to add it place the attribute `slot='icon'` on the `col-icon` element.
    <Canvas of={stories.WithIcon} />

    ### Tag Group by `col-group`
    In order to display two or more tags you should use `col-group` for expected spacing and layout.
    <Canvas of={stories.TagGroup} />

    ### Variants
    The `col-tag` component supports eight visual variants to accommodate different design contexts and user preferences.
    <Canvas of={stories.Variants} />

    ## States
    The `col-tag` component supports states to reflect user interaction. Each state is shown with all variants.

    ### Disabled
    Use the `disabled` property to render the tag non-interactive.
    <Canvas of={stories.Disabled} />

    ### Multi Select
    The `multi-select` property provides styling to the component in order see clearly which tags are selected from the ones that don't.
    <Canvas of={stories.MultiSelect} />

    ### Read Only
    The `readonly` property allows to display a tag that cannot be removed by the user, it just can be selected.
    <Canvas of={stories.ReadOnly} />

    ## Events

    ### Removal
    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      In case you see the Canva empty reload the page (or press **F5**) and the component will appear.
    </Callout>
    The `onRemove` property allows to fire a custom callback to let the parent component handle the corresponding removal of the tag.
    For this example when you click the close button, it will render other Colibri components and the possibility to re-render the tag component again.
    <Canvas of={stories.Removal} />
    - The `onRemove` callback changes the value of `isRemoved` and updates the content of the Canva wih a re-render.
    - The `click` event of the button restores the value of `isRemoved` to false and updates the content of the Canva to display the Tag.

    ## Accessibility
    <div>
      The `col-tag` can be searched up or placed under an element that needs to be classified categorically.  They can help group similar elements by color and group names.
    </div>

    - **ID Management:**
      - By default, the component generates a unique ID for the tag container and close button (e.g., `tag-container-abc123`, `tag-close-button-abc123`).
    - **Keyboard Navigation:** Fully navigable using Tab. All interactive elements (input, buttons) are accessible via keyboard.
    - **ARIA Attributes:**
      - Disabled, Read Only and Multi Select states are communicated using `aria-disabled`, `aria-readonly` and `aria-selected`.
      - The tag container includes an `aria-label` dynamic which changes according to the variant of the component.
    - **Screen Reader Support:** All states (`disabled`, `read-only`, `multi-select`) are announced appropriately.
    - **Actions Supported:** The component handles `@keydown`, `@click`, `@dragstart` and `@dragend`.
    - **Best Practices:**
      - The role of the tag component is `option` so the parent component should be `listbox` or `group`.
      - Make sure you include the `slot='icon'` in order to display correctly the icon to the tag.

    ## Properties

    <ArgTypes of={stories} />

    ## Interact with the component

    To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={stories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.

  </div>
</div>

import { html, render } from 'lit';
import { fn } from '@storybook/test';
import type { ColibriStoryMeta, ColibriStory } from '@/types/storybook';
import { formatCodeString } from '@/utils';
import { getEnumValues, TAG_VARIANTS } from '@telesign/colibri';

type StoryArgs = {
  variant: string;
  text: string;
  disabled: boolean;
  readonly?: boolean;
  category?: string;
  draggable?: boolean;
  multiselect: boolean;
  onRemove?: () => void;
  onTagSelected?: () => void;
  onTagRemoved?: () => void;
};

const variantSummary = Object.values(TAG_VARIANTS)
  .map(variant => `'${variant}'`)
  .join(' | ');

const meta = {
  title: 'Feedback/Tag',
  component: 'col-tag',
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        excludeDecorators: true,
        transform: formatCodeString,
      },
    },
  },
  argTypes: {
    text: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'The text label inside the tag.',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'Option' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'text', neq: '' },
    },
    category: {
      control: 'text',
      description: 'The category of the tag (it´s not displayed)',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: { summary: 'string' },
        defaultValue: { summary: '' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'category', neq: '' },
    },
    variant: {
      control: 'select',
      options: getEnumValues(TAG_VARIANTS),
      description: 'The visual variant of the text field.',
      table: {
        category: 'Core',
        type: {
          summary: variantSummary,
        },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'gray' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'variant', neq: '' },
    },
    disabled: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Disables the tag.',
      table: {
        category: 'State',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'disabled', neq: false },
    },
    readonly: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Makes the tag read only.',
      table: {
        category: 'State',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'readonly', neq: false },
    },
    multiselect: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Fires the styles to display a selected tag.',
      table: {
        category: 'State',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'multiselect', neq: false },
    },
    draggable: {
      control: 'boolean',
      description: 'Enables to star and end the drag of the tag.',
      table: {
        category: 'State',
        type: { summary: 'boolean' },
        defaultValue: { summary: 'false' },
      },
      if: { arg: 'draggable', neq: false },
    },
    onRemove: {
      action: 'tag-removed-callback',
      description: 'Callback function called when tag is removed via onRemove property',
      table: {
        category: 'Callback',
      },
    },
    onTagSelected: {
      action: 'tag-selected',
      description: 'Event fired when tag is selected',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
      },
    },
    onTagRemoved: {
      action: 'tag-removed',
      description: 'Event fired when tag is removed',
      table: {
        category: 'Events',
      },
    },
  },
  args: {
    variant: 'gray',
    text: 'Option',
    disabled: false,
    multiselect: false,
    readonly: false,
    draggable: false,
    category: 'Default',
    onRemove: fn(),
    onTagSelected: fn(),
    onTagRemoved: fn(),
  },
} satisfies ColibriStoryMeta<StoryArgs>;

export default meta;
type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

const renderTagWithoutIcon: Story['render'] = args =>
  html`<col-tag
    text=${args.text}
    category=${args.category}
    variant=${args.variant}
    ?disabled=${args.disabled}
    ?multiselect=${args.multiselect}
    ?readonly=${args.readonly}
    ?draggable=${args.draggable}
    .onRemove=${args.onRemove}
    @tag-selected=${args.onTagSelected}
    @tag-removed=${args.onTagRemoved}
  ></col-tag>`;

const renderTagWithIcon: Story['render'] = args =>
  html`<col-tag
    text=${args.text}
    category=${args.category}
    variant=${args.variant}
    ?disabled=${args.disabled}
    ?multiselect=${args.multiselect}
    ?readonly=${args.readonly}
    ?draggable=${args.draggable}
    .onRemove=${args.onRemove}
    @tag-selected=${args.onTagSelected}
    @tag-removed=${args.onTagRemoved}
  >
    <col-icon slot="icon" name="emoji-circle" size="16px"></col-icon>
  </col-tag>`;

export const Default: Story = {
  render: ({
    disabled,
    variant,
    text,
    multiselect,
    readonly,
    category,
    draggable,
    onRemove,
    onTagSelected,
    onTagRemoved,
  }) =>
    html` <col-tag
      text=${text}
      category=${category}
      variant=${variant}
      ?disabled=${disabled}
      ?multiselect=${multiselect}
      ?readonly=${readonly}
      ?draggable=${draggable}
      .onRemove=${onRemove}

      @tag-removed=${onTagRemoved}
    ></col-tag>`,
};

  args: {

    variant: TAG_VARIANTS.COBALT,
    readonly: true,
  },
  render: renderTagWithIcon,
};

export const Removal: Story = {
  render: () => {
    let isRemoved = false;
    let container: HTMLElement | null;
    const onRemove = () => {
      isRemoved = true;
      updateContent();
    };

    const updateContent = () => {
      if (container) {
        const newContent = isRemoved
          ? html`<col-group orientation="vertical">
              <col-typography variant="label" element="label">
                <col-icon slot="icon" name="check" size="12px"></col-icon>Tag removed successfully
              </col-typography>
              <col-button variant="primary" @click=${resetTag}>Restore Tag</col-button>
            </col-group>`
          : html`<col-tag
              variant="orange"
              text="Click my close button"
              .onRemove=${onRemove}
            ></col-tag>`;

        render(newContent, container);
      }
    };

    const resetTag = () => {
      isRemoved = false;
      updateContent();
    };

    const initialTemplate = html`<div class="story-container"></div>`;

    setTimeout(() => {
      container = document.querySelector('.story-container');
      updateContent();
    }, 0);

    return initialTemplate;
  },
  parameters: {
    docs: {
      source: {
        code: formatCodeString(`<div class="story-container">
          isRemoved
          ? <col-group orientation="vertical">
              <col-typography variant="label" element="label">
                <col-icon slot="icon" name="check" size="12px"></col-icon>Tag removed successfully
              </col-typography>
              <col-button variant="primary" @click={resetTag}>Restore Tag</col-button>
            </col-group>
          : <col-tag variant="orange" text="Click my close button" .onRemove={onRemove} ></col-tag>
          </div>`),
      },
    },
  },
};

FILE: src/components/col-text-area/col-text-area.mdx
================================================
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import { Callout } from '@/_storybook/components';
import * as TextAreaStories from './col-text-area.stories';

<Meta of={TextAreaStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Text Area</Title>
    The `col-text-area` component captures multi-line user input within forms. It extends the standard HTML `<textarea>` element with enhanced features, including integrated labels, helper text, validation states, and character counting, all while ensuring full accessibility and seamless form integration.

    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
      This component seamlessly integrates with HTML forms, supporting properties like `name` for native form participation. For more details on form integration, see the [Form Integration](#form-integration) section.
    </Callout>

    <Subtitle>Table of contents</Subtitle>
    - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
    - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
    - [Component preview](#component-preview)
    - [States](#states)
    - [Form Integration](#form-integration)
    - [Accessibility](#accessibility)
    - [Properties](#properties)
    - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

    ## How to use it
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @colibri/colibri-components
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, registerAllComponents, ColTextArea, ColTypography } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```
    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Option 1: Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColTextArea, ColTypography]);

        // Option 2: Register all Colibri components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-text-area
          id="user-comment"
          name="user-comment"
          label="Your Comment"
          placeholder="Enter your comment"
        ></col-text-area>
        ```

    ## When to use it
    The `col-text-area` component is the standard choice for multi-line text inputs. Use it when you need to:

    - **Collect Detailed Feedback**: Gather extensive user comments, reviews, or messages.
    - **Implement Descriptions**: Allow users to enter detailed descriptions or biographical information.
    - **Enforce Constraints**: Apply character limits using `charCount`.
    - **Build Forms**: Integrate seamlessly into HTML forms for data submission and validation.
    - **Provide Guidance**: Use `label`, `subLabel`, and `helper` text to guide the user.

    ## Component preview

    <div>
      The following examples showcase the core features and visual options of the `col-text-area` component.
    </div>
    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

    ### Basic Usage
    <div>
      The most fundamental text area requires only a `label` to identify its purpose.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextAreaStories.Default} />

    ### With Sub-Label
    <div>
      Use the `sub-label` property to display additional, non-critical information directly next to the main label. This is often used for marking fields as "(Optional)".
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextAreaStories.WithSubLabel} />

    ### With Helper Text
    <div>
     The `helper` property provides instructional text that appears below the input, guiding the user on the expected data format or purpose.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextAreaStories.WithHelper} />

    ### With Character Count
    <div>
      Set the `char-count` property to enforce a maximum length. The component will display a running count and prevent further input once the limit is reached.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextAreaStories.WithCharacterCount} />

    ### With Custom ID

    <div>
      This example demonstrates how to provide a custom ID for better form integration and accessibility.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextAreaStories.WithCustomId} />

  </div>
</div>

## States

<div>
  The `col-text-area` component supports several states to reflect user interaction and validation
  feedback.
</div>

### Disabled

<div>
  Use the `disabled` property to render the field non-interactive. This is useful for preventing
  user input when a field is not applicable.
</div>
<Canvas of={TextAreaStories.Disabled} />

### Readonly

<div>
  The `readonly` property allows the field to display a value that cannot be edited by the user, but
  can still be selected and copied.
</div>
<Canvas of={TextAreaStories.ReadOnly} />

### Error

<div>
  The `error` property visually marks the field as invalid and displays an error message. This is
  typically set in response to failed validation.
</div>
<Canvas of={TextAreaStories.Error} />

## Form Integration

<div>
  The `col-text-area` is designed for seamless integration with native HTML forms, supporting
  standard validation attributes and custom validation logic.
</div>

<Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
  For best accessibility and form association, provide a unique `id` for each field. This ensures
  proper label-input association and makes targeting fields in tests or scripts easier.
</Callout>

### Form Association

The component automatically associates with HTML forms using the `name` attribute. For enhanced form integration, consider using the `id` attribute:

```html
<form id="user-feedback">
  <col-text-area id="comment" name="comment" label="Comment" required></col-text-area>
</form>
```

### Validation

#### Required

<div>Add the `required` property to ensure the field must be filled before form submission.</div>
<Canvas of={TextAreaStories.Required} />

#### Minimum Length

<div>Use the `min-length` property to enforce a minimum number of characters.</div>
<Canvas of={TextAreaStories.WithMinLength} />

#### Custom Validation Message

<div>
  You can provide a custom error message using the `validation-message` property, which will be
  shown when the field is invalid.
</div>
<Canvas of={TextAreaStories.WithCustomValidationMessage} />

#### Custom Validation Timing

<div>
  Control when validation occurs by setting the `validation-timing` property to `blur` (default) or
  `input`.
</div>
<Canvas of={TextAreaStories.WithCustomValidationTiming} />

### Interactive Form Example

<div>
  The following example demonstrates how multiple `col-text-area` components work together in a
  native HTML form. It shows real-time validation, form submission, and reset behavior, with the
  submitted data displayed below the form.
</div>
<Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
  This preview shows the form structure and styling excluding the form submission and reset
  functionality. For the **full interactive experience** with working form submission, reset, and
  validation, visit the <LinkTo title={TextAreaStories.default.title} story="interactive-form-example">Canvas tab</LinkTo>.
</Callout>
<Canvas of={TextAreaStories.InteractiveFormExample} />

## Accessibility

<div>
  The `col-text-area` component is designed with accessibility in mind, ensuring that all users
  including those using assistive technologies can interact with forms effectively.
</div>

- **Labeling:** The `label` property is always associated with the input for screen readers, either via a visible label or `aria-label`.
- **Unique Identification:** Each component instance has unique IDs for all interactive elements, ensuring proper screen reader navigation.
- **ID Management:**
  - By default, the component generates a unique ID for each instance (e.g., `col-text-area-abc123`).
  - You can provide a custom `id` attribute for more predictable targeting, accessibility, and testing.
  - When a custom `id` is provided, all internal elements (label, helper, error, etc.) use that base (e.g., `my-id-label`, `my-id-helper`).
- **Keyboard Navigation:** Fully navigable using Tab/Shift+Tab.
- **ARIA Attributes:**
  - Error and validation states are communicated using `aria-invalid` and `aria-describedby`.
  - The `helper` and `error-message` properties are linked to the input for screen readers.
- **Screen Reader Support:** All states (disabled, readonly, error) are announced appropriately.
- **Best Practices:**
  - Always provide a descriptive `label`.
  - Use `helper` for additional instructions.
  - Consider providing custom `id` attributes for better form integration and accessibility.
  - Ensure custom validation messages are clear and concise.

## Properties

<ArgTypes of={TextAreaStories} />

## Interact with the component

To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={TextAreaStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.

  error: boolean;

  errorMessage?: string;

  input: () => void;
  focus: () => void;
  blur: () => void;
  keydown: () => void;
  keyup: () => void;
  paste: () => void;
  invalid: () => void;
  validationChange: () => void;
};

export default meta;
type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

const renderTextArea: Story['render'] = args => {
  const {
    id,
    name,
    value,
    label,
    subLabel,
    helper,
    placeholder,
    charCount,
    minLength,
    errorMessage,
    validationTiming,
    disabled,
    readOnly,
    error,
    required,
    change,
    input,
    focus,
    blur,
    keydown,
    keyup,
    paste,
    invalid,
    validationChange,
  } = args;

  return html`
    <col-text-area
      id=${id || nothing}
      name=${name || nothing}
      .value=${value}
      label=${label || nothing}
      sub-label=${subLabel || nothing}
      helper=${helper || nothing}
      placeholder=${placeholder || nothing}
      char-count=${charCount || nothing}
      min-length=${minLength || nothing}
      error-message=${errorMessage || nothing}
      validation-timing=${validationTiming || nothing}
      custom-width=${args.customWidth || nothing}
      ?disabled=${disabled}
      ?readonly=${readOnly}
      ?error=${error}
      ?required=${required}
      @change=${change}
      @input=${input}
      @focus=${focus}
      @blur=${blur}
      @keydown=${keydown}
      @keyup=${keyup}
      @paste=${paste}
      @invalid=${invalid}
      @validation-change=${validationChange}
    ></col-text-area>
  `;
};

export const InteractiveFormExample: Story = {
  name: 'Interactive Form Example',
  parameters: {
    controls: { disable: true },
    docs: {
      source: {
        transform: (code: string) => {
          const formMatch = code.match(/<form[^>]*slot="form"[^>]*>[\s\S]*?<\/form>/);
          return formatCodeString(formMatch?.[0] || '');
        },
      },
    },
  },
  render: () => {
    const formId = 'text-area-form-example';
    const outputId = 'text-area-form-output';
    const isInDocs = window.location.search.includes('viewMode=docs');
    const codeSnippet = hljs.highlightAuto(`
      // Handle form submit with FormValidationController
      form.addEventListener('submit', (event) => {
        // Check if validation was already prevented
        if (event.defaultPrevented) {
          console.log('Form submission blocked by validation');
          return;
        }

        // Prevent page reload
        event.preventDefault();

        // Process form data only if validation passed
        const formData = new FormData(form);
        const formValues = Object.fromEntries(formData.entries());
        console.log('Form submitted successfully:', formValues);
      });

      // Handle form reset - FormResetController handles component reset automatically
      form.addEventListener('reset', () => {
        // Only handle UI cleanup - components reset automatically
        // A custom message or action can be added here
        console.log('Form reset completed');
      });
    `).value;

    return html`
      <form-demo
        form-id="${formId}"
        output-id="${outputId}"
        title="Feedback Form"
        code-snippet="${codeSnippet}"
        code-theme="dark"
      >
        <form slot="form" id="${formId}" class="form-container">
          <col-text-area
            name="comment"
            label="Comment"
            required
            min-length="10"
            helper="Please provide a comment of at least 10 characters."
            validation-timing="input"
            custom-width="100%"
          ></col-text-area>
          <col-text-area
            name="suggestions"
            label="Suggestions"
            char-count="200"
            helper="Any suggestions for improvement? (Max 200 chars)"
            custom-width="100%"
          ></col-text-area>
          <col-group>
            <col-button type="submit" ?disabled=${isInDocs}>Submit</col-button>
            <col-button type="reset" ?disabled=${isInDocs}>Reset</col-button>
          </col-group>
        </form>
      </form-demo>
    `;
  },
};

import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta, Subtitle, Title } from '@storybook/blocks';
import LinkTo from '@storybook/addon-links/react';
import { Callout } from '@/_storybook/components';
import * as TextFieldStories from './col-text-field.stories';

<Meta of={TextFieldStories} />

<div className="col-doc__wrapper">
  <div className="col-doc__container">
    <Title>Text Field</Title>
    The `col-text-field` component is a foundational element for capturing user input within forms. It extends the standard HTML `<input>` element with enhanced features, including integrated labels, helper text, validation states, and character counting, all while ensuring full accessibility and seamless form integration.

    <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
      This component seamlessly integrates with HTML forms, supporting properties like `name` for native form participation. For more details on form integration, see the [Form Integration](#form-integration) section.
    </Callout>

    <Subtitle>Table of contents</Subtitle>
    - [How to use it](#how-to-use-it)
    - [When to use it](#when-to-use-it)
    - [Component preview](#component-preview)
    - [States](#states)
    - [Form Integration](#form-integration)
    - [Accessibility](#accessibility)
    - [Properties](#properties)
    - [Interact with the component](#interact-with-the-component)

    ## How to use it
    1. Install the packages:
        ```bash
        npm install @colibri/colibri-components
        ```
    2. Import the dependencies:
        ```typescript
        import { registerColibriComponents, registerAllComponents, ColTextField, ColTypography } from '@telesign/colibri';
        import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';
        ```

        <Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
          **Dependency Note:** The `col-text-field` component depends on the `col-typography` component for rendering labels, helper text, and error messages. Make sure to import and register `ColTypography` along with `ColTextField`.
        </Callout>

    3. Register the web components:
        ```typescript
        // Option 1: Register specific components
        registerColibriComponents([ColTextField, ColTypography]);

        // Option 2: Register all Colibri components
        registerAllComponents();
        ```
    4. Use it in your HTML:
        ```html
        <col-text-field
          id="user-name"
          name="user-name"
          label="Your Name"
          placeholder="Enter your full name"
        ></col-text-field>
        ```

    ## When to use it
    The `col-text-field` component is the standard choice for single-line text inputs. Use it when you need to:

    - **Collect Basic Information**: Gather common data like names, email addresses, or search queries.
    - **Implement Passwords**: Use the `password` type for secure password entry fields.
    - **Enforce Constraints**: Apply character limits or specific formats using `charCount` and `pattern`.
    - **Build Forms**: Integrate seamlessly into HTML forms for data submission and validation.
    - **Provide Guidance**: Use `label`, `subLabel`, and `helper` text to guide the user.

    </div>
    <Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
      You can click on the "Show code" button in the bottom-right section of the following code snippets to see how to use the component.
    </Callout>

      The most fundamental text field requires only a `label` to identify its purpose.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextFieldStories.WithLabel} />

    ### With Sub-Label

      Use the `sub-label` property to display additional, non-critical information directly next to the main label. This is often used for marking fields as "(Optional)".
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextFieldStories.WithSubLabel} />

    ### With Helper Text
    <div>
     The `helper` property provides instructional text that appears below the input, guiding the user on the expected data format or purpose.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextFieldStories.WithHelper} />

    ### With Icon

    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextFieldStories.WithIcon} />

    ### With Character Count
    <div>
      Set the `char-count` property to enforce a maximum length. The component will display a running count and prevent further input once the limit is reached.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextFieldStories.WithCharacterCount} />

    </div>


    #### Outline Variant
    <div>
      The outline variant displays a full border around the input field, providing clear visual boundaries. This is the default variant and is ideal for most form contexts where clear field separation is important.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextFieldStories.Variants} />

    #### Plain Variant
    <div>
      The plain variant removes the border entirely, creating a minimal appearance. The field only shows a border when focused or in error states, providing a cleaner, more modern look.
    </div>
    <Canvas of={TextFieldStories.PlainVariant} />
  </div>
</div>

## Form Integration

<div>
  The `col-text-field` is designed for seamless integration with native HTML forms, supporting standard validation attributes and custom validation logic.
</div>

<Callout variant="tip" icon="💡">
  For best accessibility and form association, provide a unique `id` for each field. This ensures proper label-input association and makes targeting fields in tests or scripts easier.
</Callout>

### Form Association
The component automatically associates with HTML forms using the `name` attribute. For enhanced form integration, consider using the `id` attribute:

```html
<form id="user-registration">
  <col-text-field
    id="username"
    name="username"
    label="Username"
    required
  ></col-text-field>
  <col-text-field
    id="email"
    name="email"
    label="Email"
    type="email"
    required
  ></col-text-field>
</form>
```

<div>
  Use the `min-length` property to enforce a minimum number of characters.
</div>
<Canvas of={TextFieldStories.WithMinLength} />

### Interactive Form Example
<div>
  The following example demonstrates how multiple `col-text-field` components work together in a native HTML form. It shows real-time validation, form submission, and reset behavior, with the submitted data displayed below the form.
</div>
<Callout variant="info" icon="ℹ️">
  This preview shows the form structure and styling excluding the form submission and reset functionality. For the **full interactive experience** with working form submission, reset, and validation, visit the <LinkTo title={TextFieldStories.default.title} story="interactive-form-example">Canvas tab</LinkTo>.
</Callout>
<Canvas of={TextFieldStories.InteractiveFormExample} />

## Accessibility

<div>
  The `col-text-field` component is designed with accessibility in mind, ensuring that all users including those using assistive technologies can interact with forms effectively.
</div>

- **Labeling:** The `label` property is always associated with the input for screen readers, either via a visible label or `aria-label`.
- **Unique Identification:** Each component instance has unique IDs for all interactive elements, ensuring proper screen reader navigation.
- **ID Management:**
  - By default, the component generates a unique ID for each instance (e.g., `col-text-field-abc123`).
  - You can provide a custom `id` attribute for more predictable targeting, accessibility, and testing.
  - When a custom `id` is provided, all internal elements (label, helper, error, etc.) use that base (e.g., `my-id-label`, `my-id-helper`).
- **Keyboard Navigation:** Fully navigable using Tab/Shift+Tab. All interactive elements (input, buttons) are accessible via keyboard.
- **ARIA Attributes:**
  - Error and validation states are communicated using `aria-invalid` and `aria-describedby`.
  - The `helper` and `error-message` properties are linked to the input for screen readers.
  - Clear button includes `aria-controls` and `aria-describedby` for proper association.
- **Screen Reader Support:** All states (disabled, readonly, error) are announced appropriately.
- **Best Practices:**
  - Always provide a descriptive `label`.
  - Use `helper` for additional instructions.
  - Consider providing custom `id` attributes for better form integration and accessibility.
  - Ensure custom validation messages are clear and concise.

## Properties

<ArgTypes of={TextFieldStories} />

## Interact with the component
To interact with the component, test actions, verify accessibility compliance, and perform visual testing, navigate to the <LinkTo title={TextFieldStories.default.title} story="default">Default</LinkTo> section.

type Story = ColibriStory<StoryArgs>;

const renderTextField: Story['render'] = args => html`
  <col-text-field
    id=${args.id || nothing}
    name=${args.name || nothing}
    .value=${args.value}
    label=${args.label || nothing}
    sub-label=${args.subLabel || nothing}
    helper=${args.helper || nothing}
    input-type=${args.inputType}
    variant=${args.variant}
    placeholder=${args.placeholder || nothing}
    char-count=${args.charCount || nothing}
    pattern=${args.pattern || nothing}
    min-length=${args.minLength || nothing}
    input-mode=${args.inputMode || nothing}
    error-message=${args.errorMessage || nothing}
    validation-timing=${args.validationTiming || nothing}
    custom-width=${args.customWidth || nothing}
    ?disabled=${args.disabled}
    ?readonly=${args.readOnly}
    ?error=${args.error}
    ?required=${args.required}
    @change=${args.change}
    @input=${args.input}
    @focus=${args.focus}
    @blur=${args.blur}
    @keydown=${args.keydown}
    @keyup=${args.keyup}
    @paste=${args.paste}
    @invalid=${args.invalid}
    @input-cleared=${args.inputCleared}
    @validation-change=${args.validationChange}
  >
    ${args.iconVisible
      ? html`<col-icon slot="icon" name=${args.iconName} size=${args.iconSize}></col-icon>`
      : nothing}
  </col-text-field>
`;
